<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734217 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03307: (2026x) Label layout in the diagram

- page_id: `272734217`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734217/2026x+Label+layout+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:31.748+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Layout
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Default label positions

Default label positions are retained after moving a path, shape, or related element if it is a semantically logical decision. See the example below, where association multiplicities remain at their default positions after class is moved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If labels are at their default position, reset labels position functionality is not available.

##### Label positions after moving a path, shape, or related element

After moving a path, shape, or related element, default label positions are retained if it is a semantically logical decision.

For a nicer representation of labels in a diagram, the label positions are reset to default automatically if:

1. Symbol properties are edited. When symbol properties are edited by adding or removing a label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.
2. Path, path end, or port properties are edited. When path, path end, or port data are edited by adding or removing the label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.
3. Path, shape, or related element are moved.

To check the label deviation from its default position

- Select a label and begin to move it. The dashed line shows the deviation from the default position. This helps to show the current label's owner.

When the label is not in its default position, you may indicate it by the icon on the bottom right corner, which appears after selecting the label's owner.

##### Label consideration mode

When arranging shapes for the hierarchic layout mechanisms, you may choose the **Label Consideration** mode:

- Hierarchic Layout.
- Class Diagram Layout.
- Composite Diagram Layout.
- Activity Diagram Layout.
- State Machine Diagram Layout.
- Business Process Diagram Layout.
- Ordered Hierarchic Layout.

In order to do this, you must specify the **Label consideration** mode (Full, Partial, or None) in the **Diagram Layout Options** dialog.

To specify the label consideration mode

1. From the diagram toolbar, expand [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.png'] and select Layout Options command.
2. On the left side of the dialog, select one of the following layout styles: - Under the General Layout group, select Hierarchic Layout . - Under the Specialized Layout group, select any layout style:
  - Class Diagram Layout.
  - Composite Diagram Layout.
  - Activity Diagram Layout.
  - State Machine Diagram Layout.
  - Business Process Diagram Layout.
  - Ordered Hierarchic Layout.
3. On the right side of the dialog, find the Label consideration option.
4. Click on the value of this option and select one of the following modes:
  - None - does not consider any labels, does not increase the shape size.
  - Partial - considers only the center path labels, does not increase shape sizes by labels.
  - Full - considers all labels, creates additional space for them and increases the shape sizes.
5. Click the Layout button.
6. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Default label positions</h3><p>Default label positions are retained after moving a path, shape, or related element if it is a semantically logical decision. See the example below, where association multiplicities remain at their default positions after class is moved.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Default label position example" ac:alt="Default label position example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="labels_defaul1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Label layout in the diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0ec3e34-5f2c-4d0e-8d61-db04d8ee6bef"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If labels are at their default position, reset labels position functionality is not available.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Label positions after moving a path, shape, or related element</h3><p>After moving a path, shape, or related element, default label positions are retained if it is a semantically logical decision.</p><p>For a nicer representation of labels in a diagram, the label positions are reset to default automatically if:</p><ol><li>Symbol properties are edited. When symbol properties are edited by adding or removing a label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.</li><li>Path, path end, or port properties are edited. When path, path end, or port data are edited by adding or removing the label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.</li><li>Path, shape, or related element are moved.</li></ol><p>To check the label deviation from its default position</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a label and begin to move it. The dashed line shows the deviation from the default position. This helps to show the current label's owner.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4dbb75f-65ca-4033-a7c5-0ed2722274c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the label is not in its default position, you may indicate it by the icon on the bottom right corner, which appears after selecting the label's owner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Label consideration mode</h3><p>When arranging shapes for the hierarchic layout mechanisms, you may choose the <strong>Label Consideration</strong> mode:</p><ul><li>Hierarchic Layout.</li><li>Class Diagram Layout.</li><li>Composite Diagram Layout.</li><li>Activity Diagram Layout.</li><li>State Machine Diagram Layout.</li><li>Business Process Diagram Layout.</li><li>Ordered Hierarchic Layout.</li></ul><p>In order to do this, you must specify the <strong>Label consideration</strong> mode (Full, Partial, or None) in the <strong>Diagram Layout Options</strong> dialog.</p><p>To specify the label consideration mode</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram toolbar, expand <ac:image ac:title="Quick Diagram Layout" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Quick Diagram Layout" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_diagram_layout.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Options</strong> command.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, select one of the following layout styles:<br />  - Under the <strong>General Layout</strong> group, select <strong>Hierarchic Layout</strong>.<br />  - Under the <strong>Specialized Layout</strong> group, select any layout style:<ul><li>Class Diagram Layout.</li><li>Composite Diagram Layout.</li><li>Activity Diagram Layout.</li><li>State Machine Diagram Layout.</li><li>Business Process Diagram Layout.</li><li>Ordered Hierarchic Layout.</li></ul></li><li>On the right side of the dialog, find the <strong>Label consideration</strong> option.</li><li>Click on the value of this option and select one of the following modes:<br /><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>None</strong> - does not consider any labels, does not increase the shape size.</li><li><strong>Partial</strong> - considers only the center path labels, does not increase shape sizes by labels.</li><li><strong>Full</strong> - considers all labels, creates additional space for them and increases the shape sizes.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Layout</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734233 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03308: (2026x) Label orientation

- page_id: `272734233`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734233/2026x+Label+orientation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:34.906+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Layout
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097048

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097051

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097050

You can optimize diagram layout by changing the orientation of symbol labels. Rotate labels horizontally or vertically, or select the automatic label orientation option, which adjusts labels according to the position of shapes or paths.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This sample diagrams illustrate how changing label orientation can optimize diagram layout.

To change the orientation of independent labels

1. In an open diagram, select one or several labels that you want to rotate.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: Horizontal , Vertical , or Automatic .

The labels are rotated to the selected position. If you select the **Automatic** label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of shapes or paths they are on.

To change the orientation of labels on a shape or path

1. In an open diagram, select the path or shape on which you want to rotate labels.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: Horizontal , Vertical , or Automatic .

The labels on the shape or path are rotated to the selected position. If you select the **Automatic** label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of the shape or path.

#### NOTE: Label Orientation symbol property

Label Orientation symbol property

When you change the orientation of individual labels or labels displayed on a shape or path, the value of the **Label Orientation** symbol property of the related shapes or paths changes as well. To open the **Symbol Properties** dialog, right-click a symbol in a diagram and select **Symbol Properties**.

##### Default label orientation

By default, all labels on shapes and paths are displayed horizontally. You can change the default label orientation for a project as explained below.

To change the default label orientation

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Shapes project option group.
3. On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the Label Orientation property and select one of the following values:
  - Horizontal - to display the labels of newly created shapes horizontally.
  - Vertical - to display the labels of newly created shapes vertically.
  - Automatic - to display the labels of newly created shapes according to their position in a diagram.
4. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Paths project option group.
5. On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the Label Orientation property and select one of the following values:
  - Horizontal - to display the labels of newly created paths horizontally.
  - Vertical - to display the labels of newly created paths vertically.
  - Automatic - to display the labels of newly created paths according to their position in a diagram.
6. Click OK on the bottom of the Project Options dialog.

The labels of newly created symbols in the diagrams of a project will be displayed according to your selected property values.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739828 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03309: (2026x) Launchers

- page_id: `272739828`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739828/2026x+Launchers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:01.787+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Launchers show which programs will be used for opening different file types in the application. Add new, edit, or remove launchers and specify their properties (file extension, program path or name, program arguments).

To open the **Launcher Properties** dialog

1. From the **Options** menu, choose **Environment**.
2. In the Environment Options dialog, from the options group list select **Launchers**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify properties of the default launchers

1. To edit the Default launcher, click the**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**button next to the **Default Launcher** or**Default Browser** text boxes.
2. The Launcher Properties dialog appears.
3. You can change Launcher`s executable file and arguments.

To create the defined launcher

1. Click the Add button, found below the Defined Launchers list.
2. The Launcher Properties dialog appears.
3. Specify the file extension, which will be opened with the specified launcher.
4. Type the name of the launcher in the text box, or browse to the location of the launcher by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Specify external launcher arguments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Properties are described in the following table.

| Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Extensions | Type the extensions of the files which will be opened with the specified launcher. You can define more than one extension. Separate them using commas (i.e., html, xls, doc, cpp, h). |
| Launcher | An executable file with which the extension file will be opened. |
| Arguments | Arguments are required by the external launcher, so the modeling tool provides the ability to pass some common arguments to the launcher:$f - a file name$md_dir - the modeling tool installation directory$prj_dir - the project directory (if the project is saved)$r - a row (used when opening Code Engineering object source code file with "Edit Source" action)$c - a column (used when opening Code Engineering object source code file with "Edit Source" action). |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734214 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03310: (2026x) Layout

- page_id: `272734214`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734214/2026x+Layout
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:31.357+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903811718

903811721

903811720

The diagram layout engine is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

It is easy to manage simple or complex diagrams using automated layout features that optimize diagram layout.

Symbols are automatically laid out on diagrams when displaying related elements, creating diagrams by using wizards, and creating related diagrams.

You can layout diagrams quickly by using the Quick Diagram layout. The quick layout is a recommended type of layout, and it is specific for each diagram.

The layout mechanism is built on various layout tools. There are 2 groups of layout tools: general layout tools and specific diagram layout tools. These are the general layout tools:

- Orthogonal Layout
- Hierarchic Layout
- Organic Layout
- Tree Layout
- Circular Layout
- Oblique Path Router
- Rectilinear Path Router

These layout tools are provided by yFiles layout tool component. You can arrange each diagram (except the Sequence diagram) by using any of the following general layout tools:

- Class Diagram Layout
- Composite Structure Diagram Layout
- Activity Diagram Layout
- State Machine Diagram Layout
- Business Process Diagram Layout

To layout diagrams by using the quick diagram layout, do one of the following

- Press Ctrl+Q.
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.gif'] Quick Diagram Layout button.
- On the Main menu, click Layout > Quick Diagram Layout .

To layout diagrams by using layout tools, do one of the following

- On the Main menu, click Layout and select one of the layout tools.
- On the diagram toolbar, click the small arrow next to the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.gif'] Quick Diagram Layout button, and select one of the layout tools.
- Select the element symbols you want to layout, right-click any of them, and in the shortcut menu, select Layout , then select one of the layout options.

To change the diagram layout options

1. On the Main menu, click Layout > Layout Options .
2. In the Diagram Layout Options dialog, specify the layout options.
3. Click OK .

903811717

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734630 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03311: (2026x) Layout template creation mode

- page_id: `272734630`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734630/2026x+Layout+template+creation+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:10.562+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Layout templates > (2026x) Creating layout template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating layout templates from Composite Structure Diagrams, you can select whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams (default option) or Composite Structure Diagrams. To do this, specify layout template creation mode as described below.

To specify layout template creation mode

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, expand the General node and select the General option group.
3. Click the specification cell of the Layout Template Creation Mode option on the left side of the dialog and select one of the following values as shown below:
  - Usage - if you want to create layout templates as Composite Structure Diagrams.
  - Definition - if you want to create layout templates as Class Diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying layout template creation mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating layout templates from Composite Structure Diagrams, you can select whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams (default option) or Composite Structure Diagrams. To do this, specify layout template creation mode as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify layout template creation mode</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> node and select the <strong>General</strong> option group.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Layout Template Creation Mode</strong> option on the left side of the dialog and select one of the following values as shown below:<br /><ul><li><strong>Usage</strong> - if you want to create layout templates as Composite Structure Diagrams.</li><li><strong>Definition</strong> - if you want to create layout templates as Class Diagrams.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_template_creation_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Layout template creation mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specifying layout template creation mode.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730133 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03312: (2026x) Layout templates

- page_id: `272730133`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730133/2026x+Layout+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:27.002+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The layout templates functionality, which is driven by <<layoutTemplate>> stereotype, assists you in creating well-formed diagrams. It allows you to define Class appearance once, and reuse it later when creating Parts typed by that Class and Ports in different diagrams. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED'] and their Ports are represented identically as part's type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] whose layout and style is defined in the layout template diagram. Often, Port position reflects its physical location in the system, so it is beneficial to maintain the same appearance of Parts, layout, and style of Ports throughout the whole model. In the figure below, the appearance of the *Modem Card* Block is the same in two different diagrams, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

You can reuse layout templates in other projects by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exporting packages to new projects' space='']

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Usage of a layout template. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

You can create a layout template in the Class Diagram or derive it from an existing Composite Structure Diagram as described below.

To define a layout template in the Class Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining layout template' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting diagram as layout template' space='']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying layout template' space='']

To derive a layout template for the Composite Structure Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout template creation mode' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating layout template' space='']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying layout template' space='']

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.

Read the following pages to learn how to manage layout templates:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Stopping to use diagram as layout template' space='']
- Removing applied layout template from shape
- Multiple layout template
- Setting layout template as default
- Navigation to layout templates
- Using layout templates with diagram aspects

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Composite Structure diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram aspects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exporting packages to new projects' space='']

## PAGE 03314: (2026x) Legends

- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tips for working with Legends

37096963

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tips for working with Legends

37096965

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tips for working with Legends

37096964

A Legend is a model element that allows you to define the styles of diagram symbols and table rows. It helps you to visually group model elements according to specified criteria. Use Legends to increase the readability of the diagram and table contents by highlighting important information, e.g., specific element property values or statuses. In addition, the styles applied to model elements are dynamically updated when you modify your model, so all changes are instantly visible in diagrams and tables where Legends are used.

800450

#### TIP: Tips for working with Legends

Tips for working with Legends

- You can apply multiple Legend Items to a single model element.
- You can reuse Legends in different diagrams and tables of a model, as well as in different models.

For more information about working with Legends, see the following pages:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Risk* and *Status* legends used in a Requirement Diagram.

37096962

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729215 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03315: (2026x) Length

- page_id: `272729215`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729215/2026x+Length
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:34:27.020+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) XML schemas > (2026x) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDlength. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: Length XML representation summary

Length XML representation summary

```text
<length
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = nonNegativeInteger
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</length>
```

#### PANEL: Length XML code sample

Length XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype to represent product codes which must be exactly 8 characters in length. By fixing the value of the length facet, we ensure that the type derived from productCode can change or set the values of other facets, such as pattern, but cannot change the length.
```

```text
<simpleType name= 'productCode' >
```

```text
<restriction base='string'>
```

```text
<length value='8' fixed='true'/>
```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737324 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03317: (2026x) Limiting image size

- page_id: `272737324`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737324/2026x+Limiting+image+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:31.136+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools > (2026x) Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

These functions are for limiting image size by scaling an image when its size overlaps the paper margin.

**$image.limitImageSize(image)** 
 Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page (like the Fit image to paper option).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

**$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidth, maximumHeight)**

Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified **maximumWidth** and **maximumHeight** in terms of measurements (px, in, cm, mm, pt).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
|  | maximumWidth | java.lang.String | The maximum width of the resized image in terms of measurements. |
|  | maximumHeight | java.lang.String | The maximum height of the resized image in terms of measurements. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.

******$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidthPixel, maximumHeightPixel)** 
 Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified **maximumWidthPixel** and **maximumHeightPixel** in terms of pixels.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
|  | maximumWidthPixel | int | The maximum width of the resized image in terms of pixels. |
|  | maximumHeightPixel | int | The maximum height of the resized image in terms of pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>These functions are for limiting image size by scaling an image when its size overlaps the paper margin.</p><p><br /> <strong>$image.limitImageSize(image)</strong><br /> Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page (like the Fit image to paper option).</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6b15a92-9c83-4056-8168-78192a71a472"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidth, maximumHeight)</strong></p><p>Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified <strong>maximumWidth</strong> and <strong>maximumHeight</strong> in terms of measurements (px, in, cm, mm, pt).</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumWidth</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The maximum width of the resized image in terms of measurements.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumHeight</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The maximum height of the resized image in terms of measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e69527f-1cbb-461f-80ec-d23fb3f8884d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "5in", "8in")
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "-1", "8in")
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "5in", "-1")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5378cabf-6a1d-4811-b8d1-bcbbe5cc3e04"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong><strong>$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidthPixel, maximumHeightPixel)</strong><br /> Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified <strong>maximumWidthPixel</strong> and <strong>maximumHeightPixel</strong> in terms of pixels.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumWidthPixel</td><td>int</td><td>The maximum width of the resized image in terms of pixels.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumHeightPixel</td><td>int</td><td>The maximum height of the resized image in terms of pixels.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db4c789-1956-4292-8f14-d5c9fe867096"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, 480, 680)
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, -1, 680)
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, 480, -1)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="043cfae2-7bd4-41f7-a75f-d84671a615a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738328 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03318: (2026x) Link Catching Intermediate Event

- page_id: `272738328`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738328/2026x+Link+Catching+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:46.500+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Intermediate Catch Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Link Catching Intermediate Event provides the capability to connect two sections of a process. You can use this event to either:

- Create looping situations or to avoid long sequence flow lines, as “Off-Page Connectors” to print a Process across multiple pages, or as generic Go To objects within a Process level.
- Catch a link from a Link Throwing Intermediate Event.

You can only use one Link Event for each single Process level, meaning that it cannot link a parent Process with a SubProcess.

A Link Catching Intermediate Event displays either of the following:

- A Link Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Source property value if the name is unspecified.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

****[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Link Catching Intermediate Event

**Related element**

- Intermediate Catch Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738352 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03319: (2026x) Link Throwing Intermediate Event

- page_id: `272738352`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738352/2026x+Link+Throwing+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:53.465+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Intermediate Throwing Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Link Throwing Intermediate Event is used to throw a link to a Link Catching Intermediate Event.

This event displays either of the following:

- A Link Throwing Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Target Link Event property value if the name is unspecified.

**Notation** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related element**

- Intermediate Throwing Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Event.</p><p>This event displays either of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e62fec6e-b19a-4933-b0eb-3d5d23d1543b">A Link Throwing Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.</li><li data-uuid="2f52a3af-253c-417f-86e9-ed61741eb46b">A Target Link Event property value if the name is unspecified.</li></ul><p><strong>Notation</strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="intermediate_throwing_event_link.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Link Throwing Intermediate Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="f337aea6-c71e-49ae-9ca6-18c9b3f767a0"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Intermediate+Throwing+Event">Intermediate Throwing Event</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link" data-uuid="4d91052d-e05c-4f23-8360-c35e08993ac6"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li 1358373574

1358373575

1358373574

By now you should know how to write a basic template that makes a simple reference to an element. But how will you know if the element is referenced or not? This section shows you how elements are referenced and how to call them.

1358373573

**Related pages**

## PAGE 03321: (2026x) List

- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) XML schemas > (2026x) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

- The UML Class must have the additional stereotype XSDlist.
- Binding between this class and XSD:list must be provided.
- “itemsType” maps to UML TemplateArgument from Binding.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### List model example.

#### PANEL: List XML code sample

List XML code sample

```text
<?xml version=1.0, encoding=’UTF-8’?›
```

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">	<xs:simpleType name="my_number_list2" >
```

```text
<xs:list >
```

```text
<xs:simpleType>
```

```text
<xs:restriction base="xs:string" />			</xs:simpleType›
```

```text
</xs:list>
```

```text
</xs:simpleType>
```

```text
<xs:simpleType name="my_number_list" >		<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >my list documentation</xs:documentation>		</xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:list itemType="xs:boolean" />
```

```text
</xs:simpleType>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737611 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03322: (2026x) List tool

- page_id: `272737611`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737611/2026x+List+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:49.749+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050360272

2050360275

2050360269

**On this page**

43

2050360274

$list is a list tool used to work with lists and arrays in a template. It provides a method to get and manage specified elements. You can also use it as a means to perform some actions on a list or array such as:

- Check if it is empty.
- Check if it contains certain elements.

For example:

| $primes$list.size($primes)$list.get($primes, 2)$list.set($primes, 2, 1)$list.get($primes, 2)For example:$list.isEmpty($primes)$list.contains($primes, 7) | -> new int[] {2, 3, 5, 7}-> 4-> 5-> (primes [2] becomes 1)-> 1-> false-> true |
| --- | --- |

###### **$list.contains(list, element)**

Check if a list or array contains certain elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list or array instance. |
| element | - | An element instance. |  |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if a list or array contains certain elements. Otherwise, return false. |

###### **$list.get(list, index)**

Return a specified element of a list or array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list or array instance. |
| index | - | An index of an object in a list. |  |
| Return | - | object | Return an object in a list. |

###### **$list.isArray(object)**

Check if an object is an array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | object | - | An object |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if the object is an array. Otherwise, returns false. |

###### **$list.isEmpty(list)**

Check if a list or array is empty.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if the list or array is empty. Otherwise, returns false. |

###### **$list.isList(object)**

Check if an object is a list.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | object | - | An object |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true, if the object is a List. Otherwise, return false. |

###### **$list.set(list, index, value)**

Set a specified element of a List/array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects. |
| index | - | An index of an object in the list. |  |
| value | - | An object to be set to the list. |  |
| Return | - | object | An object at the previously-specified position with the set value. |

###### **$list.size(list)**

Return the size of a List or array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects. |
| Return | - | Integer | Return the size of the list as an Integer. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Otherwise, <br />returns false.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.isEmpty(list)</strong></h4><p>Check if a list or array is empty.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td> Parameter(s)</td><td> list</td><td>- </td><td>A list of <br />objects </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true if <br />the list or array <br />is empty. Otherwise, <br />return false.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.set(list, index, value)</strong></h4><p>Set a specified element of a List/array.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">Parameter(s)   </td><td> list </td><td>- </td><td> A list of <br />objects.</td></tr><tr><td> index</td><td>-</td><td>An index of <br />an object in <br />the list. </td></tr><tr><td> value</td><td>- </td><td>An object to be <br />set to the list. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>object </td><td>An object at the <br />previously-specified <br />position with the set <br />value.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.size(list)</strong></h4><p>Return the size of a List or array.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>list </td><td>- </td><td>A list of <br />objects.</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>Integer </td><td>Return the size <br />of the list as <br />an Integer. </td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729600 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03323: (2026x) Lock View tab

- page_id: `272729600`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729600/2026x+Lock+View+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:06.634+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Lock View tab is displayed only when working with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation' space='CM'].

The Lock View tab is used to display what was locked in the project (locked elements, project structure, symbol styles) and by which users. Next to the locked element, you can see the name of the user who has locked it. Using the **Filter by user**list in the Lock View tab toolbar, you can see the elements, locked by the particular user.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Lock View tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Lock View tab.
- If the Containment tab is hidden, from the Window menu, select Lock View .

The Locked Elements tab consists of the following two components:

- Lock View tab toolbar
- Lock View tree

##### Lock View tab toolbar

The Lock View tab toolbar contains the following:

- Update Lock Information button
- Filter by user list
- Collapse All button
- Collapse Selected Recursively button

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Using the **Filter by user** list you can select to display only the elements locked by the particular user.

##### Working with model elements in the Lock View tree

You can work with elements in the Lock View tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Lock | Locks or unlocks the particular element for edit. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Inheritance Tree | Opens the Inheritance tab and selects the current element in the Inheritance tree. |

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Next to the locked element, you can see the name of the user who has locked it. Using the <strong>Filter by user </strong>list in the Lock View tab toolbar, you can see the elements, locked by the particular user.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Lock View tab" ac:alt="Lock View tab"><ri:attachment ri:filename="lock_view_tab.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To open the Lock View tab, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="35979db5-3659-490d-9153-8a9318ba74f6">At the top of the Model Browser, click the <strong>Lock View</strong> tab.</li><li data-uuid="2f809443-ffab-4a36-a1c1-101cf52b495d">If the Containment tab is hidden, from the <strong>Window</strong> menu, select <strong>Lock View</strong>.</li></ul><p>The Locked Elements tab consists of the following two components:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4d0dc675-8247-482e-be4a-ead17a193108"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Lock View tab toolbar</span></li><li data-uuid="4ed7ba9d-a100-4f60-828e-49ec96b91105"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Lock View tree</span></li></ul><h3>Lock View tab toolbar</h3><p>The Lock View tab toolbar contains the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b7f411b2-b349-4c50-8ff0-a47e725b08c5"><strong>Update Lock Information</strong> button</li><li data-uuid="3ebc772e-45b6-4bed-9628-e33e32a655e1"><strong>Filter by user</strong> list</li><li data-uuid="0f91c9c3-5408-49f8-bb03-4c9358ce6915"><strong>Collapse All </strong>button</li><li data-uuid="eb05660c-647b-4c10-88c5-eac37809aefb"><strong>Collapse Selected Recursively </strong>button</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Filter by user list" ac:alt="Filter by user list"><ri:attachment ri:filename="lock_view_filter_by_user_list.png" /></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>Using the <strong>Filter by user</strong> list you can select to display only the elements locked by the particular user.</p><h3>Working with model elements in the Lock View tree</h3><p>You can work with elements in the Lock View tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Lock</strong></p></td><td><p>Locks or unlocks the particular element for edit.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Select in Containment</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the <br />current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Select in Inheritance</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Inheritance tab and selects <br />the current element in the Inheritance tree.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="5571f32f-f404-4e11-944b-4b2f78980b29"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Model+Browser">Model Browser</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739542 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03324: (2026x) Logging background actions

- page_id: `272739542`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739542/2026x+Logging+background+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:30.002+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The UAF Background actions Logging Feature logs and displays the hidden actions performed behind the scenes while working with non-symbolic diagrams, such as Gantt Charts, in the Enterprise model. The feature aims to bring more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but not directly visible in the diagram.

The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them in the Containment tree. The feature is available for various Unified Architecture Framework diagrams in the Strategic, Services, Resources, Personnel, Projects, and Standards domains. This implementation is supported across all Enterprise models, including DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, and UAF.

For instance, in the Strategic Phasing diagram, after adding a new Capability Provision, you will be informed about the new Actual Project Milestones, Exhibits relationship, and Milestone Dependency Relationship created through the Capability Provision Creation Wizard. The user can navigate to these newly created elements and relationships from the notification window to the Containment tree.

Similarly, in the Personnel Forecast table, after filling in the relevant cells, the user will be informed about the newly created Forecast relationships for each Subject of Forecast, which they can navigate to in the Containment tree.

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Strategic Phasing diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Services Roadmap diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Personnel Forecast diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Personnel Evolution diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Resources Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='']

## PAGE 03325: (2026x) Logical viewpoint

- page_id: `272733750`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733750/2026x+Logical+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:52.724+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0
- labels: ['logical-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

821744036

821744038

821744037

The Logical viewpoint is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. The Logical viewpoint defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

821744035

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737594 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03326: (2026x) Loop tool

- page_id: `272737594`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737594/2026x+Loop+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:47.079+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

43

$LoopTool is used with the**#foreach** loops. It wraps a list to let you specify a condition to terminate the loop and reuse the same list in a different loop. The following example shows how to use **$LoopTool**.

```text
$loopTool.other
#if( $item >= 5 )$loopTool.stop()#end
#end

Output
------
1 -> 3
2 -> 4
3 -> 5
4 -> 6
5 -> 7]]>
```

###### **$LoopTool.wrap( list )**

Wrap a list with the tool.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | java.util.List | An array or a list instance. |
| Return | - | LoopTool | Return an LoopTool instance. |

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.hasMore()**

Check if the iteration has more elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if there are more elements in the wrapped list. |

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.more()**

Ask for the next element in the list.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.lang.Object | An element instance. |

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.remove()**

Remove the current element from the list.

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.reset()**

Reset the wrapper so that it will start over at the beginning of the list.

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.stop()**

Put a condition to break off a loop.

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.toString()**

Return an object as a string.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>$LoopTool is used with the<strong> #foreach</strong> loops. It wraps a list to let you specify a condition to terminate the loop and reuse the same list in a different loop. The following example shows how to use <strong>$LoopTool</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d622b74a-aa21-4514-9788-387f675e439c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set( $list = [1..7] )
#set( $others = [3..10] )
#foreach( $item in $loopTool.watch($list).sync($others, 'other') )
$item -> $loopTool.other
#if( $item >= 5 )$loopTool.stop()#end
#end

Output
------
1 -> 3
2 -> 4
3 -> 5
4 -> 6
5 -> 7]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong>$LoopTool.wrap( list )</strong></h4><p>Wrap a list with the tool.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>list </td><td>java.util.List </td><td>An array or a <br />list instance. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td>LoopTool </td><td>Return an <br />LoopTool <br />instance. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.hasMore()</strong></h4><p>Check if the iteration has more elements.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td>- </td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true if there <br />are more elements <br />in the wrapped list. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.more() </strong></h4><p>Ask for the next element in the list.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td>- </td><td> java.lang.Object </td><td>An element <br />instance. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.remove() </strong></h4><p>Remove the current element from the list.</p><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.reset() </strong></h4><p>Reset the wrapper so that it will start over at the beginning of the list.</p><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.stop()</strong></h4><p>Put a condition to break off a loop.</p><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.toString()</strong></h4><p>Return an object as a string.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733870 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03327: (2026x) Lr Lines of Development

- page_id: `272733870`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733870/2026x+Lr+Lines+of+Development
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:08.240+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['lr-lines-of-development', 'lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

67572108

67572110

67572109

**Description**

The Lr Lines of Development view specifies the logical threads (lines of development) for a set of projects and programmes. The status of each of these threads can be shown at the various milestones in the projects or programmes. The Lr view is primarily intended to support the acquisition process across multiple projects or programmes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

Use of the Lr view should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap' space='']Capability Roadmapview.

The Lr view may be used for:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependencies and the identification of associated risk.
- Portfolio management.
- Through Life Management Planning (TLMP).

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix . [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_to_capability.png']
- Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_portfolio.png']

- Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart). [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_programme_to_capability_mapping.png']
- Lr Responsibility Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_matrix.png']

67572107

The following pages present detailed instructions on working with macros using Macro Engine. Macro Engine enables you to easily create, add, and edit your macros to customize them to your needs.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729893 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03329: (2026x) Macro keyboard shortcuts

- page_id: `272729893`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729893/2026x+Macro+keyboard+shortcuts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:59.338+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Macro Engine allows you to assign keyboard shortcuts to the macros that you have created using either the **Environment Options** or **Macro Information** dialog, see figures below. You can later press the keyboard shortcuts to execute or run the macros.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

To open the keyboard shortcuts pane in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. Click Options > Environment on the main menu. The Environment Options dialog will open.
2. Click Keyboard, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The macro information and keyboard shortcuts that are saved either in the**Environment Options** or **Macro Information** dialog will be stored as a modeling tool's environment. They will not be kept in a specific project file [**.mdzip*].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Macro Information or Environment Options dialog will be disabled until you type a keyboard shortcut in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box.

The table below lists the Keyboard Shortcut Buttons

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | To assign a new keyboard shortcut to a macro. |
|  | To remove the keyboard shortcuts from the selected item in box. |
|  | To remove all keyboard shortcuts from a macro. |

The table below lists the Keyboard Shortcut Text Boxes

| Field | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Current keys | To store a list of keyboard shortcuts currently assigned to a macro. |
|  | To type a new keyboard shortcut to be assigned to a macro. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737922 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03330: (2026x) Main menu

- page_id: `272737922`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737922/2026x+Main+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:23.221+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['file-menu', 'edit-menu', 'view-menu', 'layout-menu', 'diagrams-menu', 'options-menu', 'tools-menu', 'analyze-menu', 'collaborate-menu', 'window-menu', 'help-menu', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following elements:

| Menu element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File | Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., , , and . Among other actions, this menu also allows you to import data from external sources, export a project, or . |
| Edit | Use this menu when working with diagrams on the Diagram pane. The Edit menu contains the commands for selecting, symbols or entire diagrams, and finding elements in the current project. |
| View | Contains commands allowing you to of a diagram, use and customize a , manage diagram tabs (if you have several open diagrams) and of your modeling tool. |
| Layout | Use this menu for managing the of the symbols on an active diagram. To enable certain Layout menu commands, you must select more than one symbol. |
| Diagrams | Allows you to , access diagram wizards and history, and . |
| Options | Use this menu to specify and , select or , and choose the style for your modeling tool. |
| Tools | Contains the set of commands allowing you to execute for converting models between different modeling domains, , on the actual testing model, generate code for the selected model data, create and organize , access the Cameo DataHub Plugin, and more. |
| Analyze | Use this menu to access the, create implementation matrices, analyze and show model dependencies, , , or , and perform other model analysis-related actions. |
| Collaborate | Contains the commands for . |
| Window | Allows you to manage the layout of different windows in your modeling tool. |
| Help | Among other actions, this menu allows opening online documentation, managing resources, and accessing modeling tool-related information. |

#### NOTE: Customizable menus

Customizable menus

You can choose which of these menus are shown/hidden by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing Perspectives' space=''].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Toolbars' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Image Library tab' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732061 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03331: (2026x) Maintaining decomposed model integrity

- page_id: `272732061`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732061/2026x+Maintaining+decomposed+model+integrity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:13.193+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Project partitioning
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To maintain model integrity, there are two types of elements appearing in the model:

- Proxies
- Recovered elements

INLINE

A **proxy** is a lightweight surrogate that carries only the name and kind of the model element information. These proxies appear in the place of the original used project elements, and are dedicated to maintaining model integrity (so that there are no dangling ends of relationships, types of properties do not disappear, and so on), while the used project is not accessible (for example, you do not have permissions) or is not loaded.

When you do not have granted “Read model” permission for a used project, this used project will not be loaded in a project where it is being used. To maintain the model integrity and keep model references that point to elements of “not accessible” used project, proxies are being displayed instead of referenced not accessible elements. That means that you will be able to see only the following information of these elements:

- Metaclasses of the element
- Names of the element
- Owners of the element

Recovered element is really an indication of the dangling reference. Appearance of these elements indicates that there are elements in the project (for example, elements in the main project or its used projects) that refer to the element in the used project that was previously there but currently cannot be found. It may be that the element was unsafely deleted/ removed/somehow made unavailable in the module. In such case, a modeling tool continues maintaining model integrity of unresolved reference by recovering missing elements - so called “Recovered element” appear in a place of the missing element where the original element existed in the past.

Read how to manage recovered elements in the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To maintain model integrity, there are two types of elements appearing in the model:</p><ul><li>Proxies</li><li><span>Recovered elements</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7ee982-c055-4a89-a01d-0c05525a829b"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <strong>proxy</strong> is a lightweight surrogate that carries only the name and kind of the model element information. These proxies appear in the place of the original used project elements, and are dedicated to maintaining model integrity (so that there are no dangling ends of relationships, types of properties do not disappear, and so on), while the used project is not accessible (for example, you do not have permissions) or is not loaded.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>When you do not have granted “Read model” permission for a used project, this used project will not be loaded in a project where it is being used. To maintain the model integrity and keep model references that point to elements of “not accessible” used project, proxies are being displayed instead of referenced not accessible elements. That means that you will be able to see only the following information of these elements:</p><ul><li>Metaclasses of the element</li><li>Names of the element</li><li>Owners of the element</li></ul><p>Recovered element is really an indication of the dangling reference. Appearance of these elements indicates that there are elements in the project (for example, elements in the main project or its used projects) that refer to the element in the used project that was previously there but currently cannot be found. It may be that the element was unsafely deleted/ removed/somehow made unavailable in the module. In such case, a modeling tool continues maintaining model integrity of unresolved reference by recovering missing elements - so called “Recovered element” appear in a place of the missing element where the original element existed in the past.</p><p>Read how to manage recovered elements in the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="320d573d-cb09-4e14-9949-93db34336a46" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734126 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03332: (2026x) Making the paths corners rounded

- page_id: `272734126`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734126/2026x+Making+the+paths+corners+rounded
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:20.352+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['path-corners-rounded', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To make the path corners rounded

1. Right-click the path and click Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Rounded Corners property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To make the path corners rounded</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the path and click <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set  the <strong>Rounded Corners</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734723 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03333: (2026x) Making your own default style for new projects

- page_id: `272734723`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734723/2026x+Making+your+own+default+style+for+new+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:21.427+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2042041905

2042041917

2042041907

When you modify symbol styles in a projects, these styles will be available only in that project. However, you can make your own default symbol styles and use them for the new projects. In order to do that, you will be needing to modify the template.

To modify the template

1. From the <modeling tool installation directory>\templates , open the template.mdzip .
2. In the Project Options dialog, modify symbol styles.
3. Save the template.

You can create and define your own project and use it as template.

After you define your desired symbol styles, you can use them in your new projects. All you need to do is to create project from template.

To create a project from template

1. Do one of the following:
  - From the File menu, select New Project .
  - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
  - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.
2. Select the Project from Template in the Other domain.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Select your modified template.
5. Click OK .

2042041903

**Related Pages**

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Formatting symbols' space='']

However, you can make your own default symbol styles and use them for the new projects. In order to do that, you will be needing to modify the template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To modify the template</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\templates</em>, open the <em>template.mdzip</em>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, modify symbol styles.</li><li>Save the template.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d5367f9-f03f-4fd9-8067-20a44d289915"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create and define your own project and use it as template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>After you define your desired symbol styles, you can use them in your new projects. All you need to do is to create project from template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project from template</p><hr /><ol><li class="atl-forced-newline">Do one of the following:<ul><li class="atl-forced-newline">From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>New Project</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">On the main toolbar, click the <strong>New Project</strong> button.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Press Ctrl + Shift + N.</li></ul></li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Select the <strong>Project from Template</strong> in the <strong>Other</strong> domain.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Specify name and location.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Select your modified template.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b5b8629-665f-418b-87db-bd725655d348"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2042041903</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8726d51c-719f-48b6-a9bc-6c2acbeb3067"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734870 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03334: (2026x) Manage favorites

- page_id: `272734870`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734870/2026x+Manage+favorites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:54.405+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Favorites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1542432437

1542432449

1542432439

The Manage Favorites dialog allows:

- Adding to favorites
- Removing from favorites
- Changing the order of favorites

To open the **Manage Favorites** dialog

- In the Model Browser, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] button (on the toolbar of the open tab), and then select Manage Favorites . [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_open_manage_fav_dlg.png']

The **Manage Favorites** dialog is a modification of the element Selection dialog. The dialog displays:

- All elements (in tree or list view on the left side of the dialog)
- Favorite elements (in the list on the right side of the dialog)
- Buttons for adding and removing from favorites
- Search modes and scope filters area
- Buttons for reordering favorites

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The order of favorites in the list of the **Manage Favorites** dialog meets the order of favorites on the Favorites menu. Thus, if you need to change the order of favorites on this menu, make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the **Manage Favorites** dialog. Buttons below the list will help you change the order of favorites.

1542432426

**Related pages**

- Favorites

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff7555ec-f21b-40ac-96be-4f7701029273"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1542432437</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64c8acd1-199a-4d31-8eba-eb08cb5bc618"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1542432449</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54078e9d-4264-42cd-b52f-16c8c5a6cfde"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1542432439</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Manage Favorites dialog allows:</p><ul><li>Adding to favorites</li><li>Removing from favorites</li><li>Changing the order of favorites</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Manage Favorites</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Model Browser, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Manage favorites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>button (on the toolbar of the open tab), and then select <strong>Manage Favorites</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites_open_manage_fav_dlg.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Manage favorites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Manage Favorites</strong> dialog is a modification of the element Selection dialog. The dialog displays:</p><ul><li>All elements (in tree or list view on the left side of the dialog)</li><li>Favorite elements (in the list on the right side of the dialog)</li><li>Buttons for adding and removing from favorites</li><li>Search modes and scope filters area</li><li>Buttons for reordering favorites</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_favorites_dialog_updated.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Manage favorites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The order of favorites in the list of the <strong>Manage Favorites</strong> dialog meets the order of favorites on the Favorites menu. Thus, if you need to change the order of favorites on this menu, make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the <strong>Manage Favorites</strong> dialog. Buttons below the list will help you change the order of favorites. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b52bd24a-84b3-4ab0-bc71-aacb87f1f092"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1542432426</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Favorites">Favorites</a> </li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
