<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734826 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03335: (2026x) Manage Navigation

- page_id: `272734826`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734826/2026x+Manage+Navigation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:32.551+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1761606203

1761606207

1761606206

You can define and manage navigation from the element. Here is what you can do:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space=''] .
- Set the active navigation target. You can choose which target should be opened when you double-click the element.
- Navigate to usages in owned diagrams and tables.
- View traceability, instances, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing navigation

One element can have more than one target (hyperlink to web page, element, diagram, file, etc.) assigned, but you can choose which one should be set as an active target. The target which will be set as active is the one that opens when you double-click the element.

If the element has an active navigation target then an active navigation target icon is shown on a symbol. You can choose whether to show the icon

- by selecting the **Show Active Navigation Icons** command from the diagram toolbar Options. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- by changing a value of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space=''] **Show Active Navigation Icons**. By default, this option is set to *true*.

In the **Navigation/Hyperlinks** property group, you will find groups of suggestions for the active navigation target. You can choose from the suggestions of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining hyperlinks' space='']. The Default Navigation property means, that active target is the one defined by our rules. For example, for the Block the SysML v1 Internal Block Diagram will be opened.

1761606202

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

## PAGE 03336: (2026x) Managing Block Interfaces

- page_id: `272732962`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732962/2026x+Managing+Block+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:35.451+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x) Defining Interface Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All owned and inherited ports and their [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] of the selected [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] are collected on the left of the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] > **Ports/Interfaces**. Manage them by creating, redefining or deleting.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Direction | Direction prefix of the port. The tilde symbol (~) appears before the direction prefix when the port is conjugated. >]]> |
| Port Name | Name of the port. |
| Port Type | Type of the port. |
| Type Features | Features of the port type. |

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the Specification window of the selected port. |
| Create | Opens the list with the available to create properties. Click to create the , , , , , . |
| Redefine | Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order. |
| Delete | Removes the selected item from the list. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734732 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03337: (2026x) Managing Cr diagram

- page_id: `272734732`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734732/2026x+Managing+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:22.918+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in CV-3 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space=''] when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Removing Capabilities

To remove a Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Removing Sub Capabilities

To remove a Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can manage the data in CV-3 diagram in the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7646c25c-092e-417e-86b2-4b3149ae1151" /></p><h3>Adding Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><h3>Removing Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To remove a Sub Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> Remove From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Sub Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739425 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03338: (2026x) Managing CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `272739425`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739425/2026x+Managing+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:22.272+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in CV-3 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space=''] when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Deleting Capabilities and Sub Capabilities

To remove a Capability or Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability or Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability or Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability or Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729013 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03339: (2026x) Managing derived SysML properties and tags

- page_id: `272729013`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729013/2026x+Managing+derived+SysML+properties+and+tags
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:21.344+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Functional analysis > (2026x) Functional Allocations
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All derived SysML properties and tags are collected in the **Allocations** property group.

To add or remove an element

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] on the left.
2. Select an element from the Select Elements dialog.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_element_button.png'] to add element / [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_element_button.png'] to remove element.
4. Click OK .

The corresponding relationships are created and collected in the **Relations**property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Allocated From | Elements that are allocated to this element. |
| Allocated To | Elements the selected element is allocated to. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>All derived SysML properties and tags are collected in the <strong>Allocations</strong> property group.</p><p>To add or remove an element</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the left.</li><li>Select an element from the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Add" ac:alt="Add"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to add element /<ac:image ac:title="Remove" ac:alt="Remove"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to remove element.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The corresponding relationships are created and collected in the <strong>Relations </strong>property group.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Allocations property group" ac:alt="Allocations property group"><ri:attachment ri:filename="allocation_property_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing derived SysML properties and tags" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Column name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Allocated From</strong></td><td><p>Elements that are allocated to this element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Allocated To</strong></td><td><p>Elements the selected element is allocated to.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732323 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03340: (2026x) Managing element groups

- page_id: `272732323`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732323/2026x+Managing+element+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:26.888+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create element groups to more easily manage selected (possibly heterogeneous) model elements. For example, you can group elements associated with a particular model release, elements that have a certain risk level, or requirements that need to be satisfied. New elements can be added to a group and unnecessary ones removed from it any time directly from the element shortcut menu.

To create an element group

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an element.
2. Click Element Group > Create Group . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_element_group.png']
3. Name the newly created group and specify other properties. [ATTACHMENT filename='element_group_specification_window.png']
4. Click Close when you are done. The element group is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='created_element_group.png']

To add an element to an element group

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an element and then go to Element Group .
2. Check the box next to the group you want to add an element to. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_element_to_group.png']

To delete an element from an element group

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an element.
2. Uncheck the box next to the group you want to remove an element from. [ATTACHMENT filename='removing_element_from_group.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create element groups to more easily manage selected (possibly heterogeneous) model elements. For example, you can group elements associated with a particular model release, elements that have a certain risk level, or requirements that need to be satisfied. New elements can be added to a group and unnecessary ones removed from it any time directly from the element shortcut menu.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click an element.</li><li>Click <strong>Element Group</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Group</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_element_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Name the newly created group and specify other properties.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_group_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> when you are done. The element group is created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="created_element_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an element to an element group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click an element and then go to <strong>Element Group</strong>.</li><li>Check the box next to the group you want to add an element to.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_element_to_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To delete an element from an element group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click an element.</li><li>Uncheck the box next to the group you want to remove an element from. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_element_from_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734593 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03341: (2026x) Managing Excel and CSV file

- page_id: `272734593`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734593/2026x+Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:06.895+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Tables > (2026x) Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using UML properties upon importUsing UML properties upon import

If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.

To use UML properties upon import

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import

By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.

- If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).

To skip empty rows/columns via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the numberEmptyRowsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.
5. Select the numberEmptyColumnsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
6. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.
7. Click **Close**.

To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the **JAVA_ARGS** line, add the following values, and replace *POSITIVE_NUMBER* with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### To export by IDMapping elements by ID upon export

By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.

If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.

To map elements by ID via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **exportByIdentificationProperty** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To map elements by ID via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Color marking modified data upon export

If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.

- If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- To apply color marking, mapping elements by ID must be enabled first.
- Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon Read From File action.

To color mark modified data via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **markExportedRows** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To color mark modified data via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:

- Red - removed
- Blue - updated
- Green - added

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table

To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Open File . The linked file is opened.

##### Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table

If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created.

To remove it, follow the procedure below.

To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Clear Selection . The reference between tables is removed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3ca1be4-4bfd-4ac4-a218-4d3e42aeac48"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Using UML properties upon import</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Using UML properties upon import</h3><p>If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.</p><p><br />To use UML properties upon import</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9e67b198-1f6e-483e-b76b-9e0cc1b2ced5">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="c940a86b-93db-4c53-a0a1-c7fb8c5f5d21">In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li data-uuid="8c073352-213c-4b92-80ba-b3a8f73a7c51">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import</h3><p>By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b2d6e3a-8c64-4300-99b1-5479c4f00fe4"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="fbcfcb65-e491-4ffe-8aa5-c0c48687b3e3">If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li data-uuid="a8db3a98-4bc3-40c3-84ed-d6af23451440">The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To skip empty rows/columns via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2875442d-f4d8-4a70-9a7e-faa622322b13">Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li data-uuid="bd308543-d771-45a7-8fd5-a62afe845d24">In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="26f9e7d6-7efd-4a49-a759-45ad10ec3a53">Select the <strong>numberEmptyRowsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="fb5656e2-d7ec-4842-827c-b8d4a4f986fc"><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.</span></li><li data-uuid="2b9852eb-1b48-4310-b7b2-fe9380af3f39">Select the <strong>numberEmptyColumnsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource»  stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="afb64d4e-6981-4a81-b584-2e38bea445b2"><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.</span></li><li data-uuid="525ef3b3-a0b0-4ba3-a0f3-5e179a9d82f6"><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5c65b9c3-e54d-423a-9862-ad9626d61354">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="4040fc6a-22cb-4f20-884e-befd59c2e5aa"><p>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line, add the following values, and replace <span><em>POSITIVE_NUMBER</em> with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8d1721b-4d65-424e-b438-1d455094bac5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dnumber.empty.rows.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER
-Dnumber.empty.columns.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="bfa8e3ec-9b7d-45e3-a0a5-64a49d025bb6">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72ac8d2e-7190-4ed9-bbc8-ab71db7240b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">To export by ID</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Mapping elements by ID upon export</h3><p>By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="998aa366-976e-4d34-8afa-5cf0b8c1d789"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To map elements by ID via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a5c8c6f4-73af-46ed-aed7-3ee8f3b08b54">Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li data-uuid="aab80719-93cf-4a96-84b8-d87d64dbb104">In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="cbc9274d-af6a-4ebe-9051-f40e6fd50ef3">Select the <span><strong>exportByIdentificationProperty</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button. </span></li><li data-uuid="23ad2fae-50a3-4479-9207-21d8817e301e"><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="0808ccc4-9fef-4ff8-b9ef-a5d9030121a7"><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To map elements by ID via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="bf5c5cd3-5e07-4342-ab54-4ec80c382e90">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="7f21d902-239d-4aa5-a4a5-322131ac008c">In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li data-uuid="5e9b78db-0664-4a5b-ac0a-b0aab1fa7deb">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Color marking modified data upon export</h3><p>If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a294723e-2a8e-428f-a27b-dd6198b28341"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="1622fcb9-b35f-4dc2-a69c-c123aa4cc2c3">If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li data-uuid="d028258e-60d5-4838-80e4-a0322257ee0d">To apply color marking, <strong> mapping elements by ID </strong>must be enabled first.</li><li data-uuid="8ec20716-4b0f-4f25-93db-e7a28f92b8fc">Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon <strong>Read From File</strong> action. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To color mark modified data via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5b6edbb4-9b18-4a7f-bcd1-4ce3db91e3b1">Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li data-uuid="7d4e5435-2d9f-4772-bb47-9aec8a12fd52">In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="3f55f3d4-b4be-43eb-b4ee-974d2b6dbed6">Select the <span><strong>markExportedRows</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="c8768116-b897-48e6-9c21-7f46df929cd9"><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="896d86d9-e319-4932-8a05-859b03092dca"><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To color mark modified data via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="50b362bf-5212-4647-9761-6e6e2a2e71a9">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="3f9520b9-d16f-4f26-8adc-639ab002ef08">In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li data-uuid="5f6e6418-44db-4de0-b928-157d6097432e">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br />As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8d18af91-3ffe-4bd8-80bc-5b4225b78eac">Red - removed</li><li data-uuid="08d389bb-4bda-4767-a155-66687a94045a">Blue - updated</li><li data-uuid="406d57b0-ebb6-4491-a19a-52636c925025">Green - added</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking_exported_data.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><span>Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table</span></h3><p>To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a6cc169d-0ec6-4345-b0ef-8ae3aa23f12a">On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="bcdb8c3d-3592-4c21-bdd0-2c4ad71dc71c">Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open File</strong>.<br />The linked file is opened.</li></ol><h3>Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To remove it, follow the procedure below. </span></p><p>To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d535d55e-e1bf-44d8-b3a0-89600fa98b04">On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="4d5a3539-920d-46d7-b0d6-199b5e6d42ba">Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Clear Selection</strong>.<br />The reference between tables is removed.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732807 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03342: (2026x) Managing Interfaces of the Proxy Port

- page_id: `272732807`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732807/2026x+Managing+Interfaces+of+the+Proxy+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:15.699+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x) Defining Interface Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The detailed information about Proxy Port interfaces is collected on the left of the Port [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] > **Interface Block Properties**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Interface Block properties group in the Specification window of the Proxy Port.

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Compartment name. |
| Type | Compartment type. |
| Default Value | Use to set the value manually. |
| Owner | which is the owner of the selected. |

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the of the compartment |
| Up | Move item to upper position in the list. The items are automatically renumbered after moving. |
| Down | Move item to lower position in the list. The items are automatically renumbered after moving. |
| Create | Opens the list with the available to create properties. Click to create the , or . |
| Redefine | Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order. |
| Delete | Removes the selected item from the list. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<p>The detailed information about Proxy Port interfaces is collected on the left of the Port <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Interface Block Properties</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interface_block_properties_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Interfaces of the Proxy Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Interface Block properties group in the Specification window of the Proxy Port.</h6><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Column name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>Compartment name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>Compartment type.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Default Value</strong></td><td>Use to set the value manually.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Owner</strong></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface Block" /></ac:link> which is the owner of the selected<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Proxy Port]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Open Specification" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Open Specification" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Interfaces of the Proxy Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Opens the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link> of the compartment</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Up</strong></td><td><p>Move item to upper position in the list. The items are automatically renumbered after moving.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Down</strong></td><td><p>Move item to lower position in the list. The items are automatically renumbered after moving.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Create</strong></td><td><p>Opens the list with the available to create properties. Click to create the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Property" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Flow Property" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Reference Property" /></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Redefine</strong></td><td>Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Delete</strong></td><td>Removes the selected item from the list.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730371 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03343: (2026x) Managing Map Groups

- page_id: `272730371`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730371/2026x+Managing+Map+Groups
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:32.855+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the Map Groups is to simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>

It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.

The following figure demonstrates the *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group that contains *CarBreak Requirments*, *CarBreak Structure* and *Car Break Satisfy*Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

##### Creating Map Groups

To create a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups .
2. In the **Tree** tab of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog, select the *Excel/CSV imports*Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Specification window opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Name** property value, type the name for the new Map Group and click the **Close** button.

The map group is created and becomes visible in the **Select Map Group for Editing**drop-down list box of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.

##### Deleting Map Groups

To delete the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups .
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. Click the **Delete**button. The Map Group is deleted.

##### Adding import maps to a Map Group

To add Import Maps to a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']to add only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add it recursively.

The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group

##### Removing import maps from a Map Group

To remove an Import Map from a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to remove.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove them recursively.

The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.

##### Reordering import maps in the Map Group

By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.

To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.
4. Click **Up** to move the selected Import Map up or click **Down** to move the Import Map down.

The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']
- Excel and CSV Import dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of the Map Groups is to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually</span>. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to save Import Map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0371a7b-7feb-4cf2-b478-dcb3347cfefc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure demonstrates the <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group that contains <em>CarBreak Requirments</em>, <em>CarBreak Structure</em> and <em>Car Break Satisfy </em>Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_map_group_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</h6><h3>Creating Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f2a810cd-3e3e-4af9-90c6-81b1f9c380c3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="a6fc0556-83bb-43bc-9283-f0fa4fb45eb4"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog, select the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>Excel/CSV imports</em> </span>Package as the owner for the new Map Group.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="c4927c38-d351-43a0-9054-0c98e984610b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">he Specification window opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_group_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></li><li data-uuid="4a933019-cc48-4563-8ae4-3b2a98d2d2a2"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Name</strong> property value, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">type the name for the new Map Group and click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</span></span></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The map group is created and becomes visible in the <strong>Select Map Group for Editing </strong>drop-down list box of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Deleting Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To delete the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Map Group</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f60dcc58-12ac-4615-a1d7-29cd58d4ba27"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="8e25b4f4-59b1-4909-b783-b8b18dad65a5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="e1fbda9a-bf56-4412-b9d0-8466e682e536"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Map Group is deleted.</span></li></ol><h3>Adding import maps to a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To add Import Maps to a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e13639d0-ce18-4f28-8876-fff3cb186c65"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li data-uuid="e83d5014-2844-4eda-b3c8-59fd8e9b3206"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="5773f120-3bd6-48a0-a1bb-6180f27ac66f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="c1bd9312-13d3-43a1-adfb-abcfec7d62c9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to add only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_recursively_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add it recursively.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group</span></p><h3>Removing import maps from a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To remove an Import Map from a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0acd57da-dd2a-4cc2-9a05-4ec6aa9b2e5e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. T<span>he </span><strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong><span> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="ddaa97a3-1a49-4eb3-b943-95dfd4741655"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="76ea0454-86ed-4d07-ae6b-4734a43eb966"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Map Groups</strong> area, select the Import Map you want to remove.</span></li><li data-uuid="7f4e4e44-86ae-4d0c-8f27-0c118f215aae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to remove only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_all_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to remove them recursively.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.</span></p><h3>Reordering import maps in the Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.</p><p>To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="52092201-802b-45ae-9ccc-975bcbd59cc3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li data-uuid="3a1f5de5-5843-4e24-a643-cdbc4cf77787"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="383ab340-e0aa-4152-84c3-1f4a11bd06e0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Map Groups</strong> area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.</span></li><li data-uuid="a2e187ac-bf1c-4b77-8d37-026480f1ea8b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>Up</strong> to move the selected Import Map up or click <strong>Down</strong> to move the Import Map down.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li data-uuid="12bff927-6f6e-4b5d-adac-f7e191178232"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739565 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03345: (2026x) Managing project properties

- page_id: `272739565`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739565/2026x+Managing+project+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:32.072+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['architectural-description-element', 'root-element', 'manage-project-properties', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Architectural description as the root element" ac:title="Architectural description as the root element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architectural_description.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:alt="Architectural Description Specification window" ac:title="Architectural Description Specification window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architectural_description_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739791 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03346: (2026x) Managing PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739791`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739791/2026x+Managing+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:58.165+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in PV-2 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Type its name
5. Specify Start Date and End Date for the Actual Sub Project.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in PV-2 diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### editingEditing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in PV-2 diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Deleting Actual Projects

To delete a Actual Project from the chart

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Actual Project from the model

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### removeRemoving Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] step 2 opens.
2. In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select the relation type:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> <br /></span></li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Deleting Actual Project Milestones</h3><p>To delete an Actual Project Milestone</p><hr /><ul><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Deleting Actual Projects</h3><p>To delete a Actual Project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Actual Project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Actual Project from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Actual Project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccc8194f-b1c8-4e01-8287-cd83bd4f1614"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing Project Status</h3><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739509 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03348: (2026x) Managing tables

- page_id: `272739509`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739509/2026x+Managing+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:27.125+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['table-toolbar', 'data-table', 'add-new', 'add-existing', 'delete-from-table', 'delete', 'default-owner', 'move-up', 'move-down', 'show-columns', 'report', 'show-full-types', 'show-rows', 'manage-tables', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216584849

216584851

216584850

The purpose of the tables is to improve usability of particular UAF products that cannot be achieved by using custom diagrams or matrices. Tables can be created from diagrams menu exactly the same as diagrams. All the tables in UAF plugin are editable.

The table pane consists of two major parts:

1. Toolbar . It's position is fixed at the top of the table.
2. Data Table. It is right below the toolbar.

These parts are arranged horizontally.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Toolbar buttons are quite similar for all tables. There are the **Add****New**, **Add****Existing**, **Delete****from****Table**, **Delete**, **Default****Owner**, **Move****Up** and **Move****Down**, **Show****Columns**, **Report**, **Export**, and **Show****Full****Types** buttons on the toolbar. Other buttons that are specific to particular product are described within the product specification sections.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add New | To create a new element in the model and a new row in the data table. |
| Add Existing | To allow you choosing one or more existing elements from the model and add a new rows to the data table. |
| Delete from Table | To remove the selected element and row from the data table. |
| Delete | To remove the selected element and row from the data table and the model. |
| Default Owner | To define Constrains and Measurement Sets default ownership. |
| Move Up | To move the selected row up. |
| Move Down | To move the selected row down. |
| Show Columns | To open the shortcut menu with a list of the available columns for the data table.You will find that currently displayed columns are selected.Click an unselected column to display it.Click a selected column to hide it. |
| Report | To create a corresponding spreadsheet report by passing all the elements form the data table directly to the report. The Report button is available for CV-5, OV-3 (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), OV-6a (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), SV-6, SV-7, SV-10a, SOV-4a, SvcV-7, and SvcV-10a, StV-5 tables. You can generate either an OpenDocument (.ods) or Microsoft Excel 2007 (.xslx) spreadsheet report. |
| Export | To export an active table either to a plain text file (.cvs) or to a Hypertext Markup Language (.html) format. |
| Show Full Types | To show or hide the full path (qualified name) of each element used in the data table. |

Tables are fully synchronized with model which means that modification to the table such as deleting or creating will affect model elements except for a few cases that are described within the product specification sections. 
 
 Some tables, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], allow rows filtering. 
 
 To filter row

1. Create an OV-3 or SV-6 table.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Show Rows button.
3. Deselect row types that you want to hide or select row types that you want to show in the table.

216584848

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tables' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732574 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03350: (2026x) Managing unresolved references

- page_id: `272732574`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732574/2026x+Managing+unresolved+references
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:48.505+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Project partitioning > (2026x) Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1525838642

INLINE

1525838654

INLINE

1526101509

INLINE

**On this page**

3

1525838644

INLINE

An unresolved reference means that your project contains an element that refers to some used project element that has become inaccessible. This can happen because the project element is no longer shared, it was removed, it was moved to a non-shared location, etc. To maintain your model integrity and project composition when the element is inaccessible, the modeling tool recovers the inaccessible elements to maintain the references that are indicated in the project as unresolved.

##### Running composition integrity analysis

If recovered elements are detected, a summary of validation errors is shown in the status line. You can run the composition integrity analysis to find and examine the usages of the recovered elements in your project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A summary of validation errors in the status line.

To run the composition integrity analysis

1. Do either: The **Validation Results** panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis In the **Active Validation Results** panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is REF or NRAPU. From its shortcut menu, select **Run Composition Integrity Analysis**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis in Model BrowserIn the Model Browser, right-click an el ement that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu, select Validation > Recovered Element > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='composition_integrity_analysis_from_model_browser.png']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element shape that refers to the recovered element. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png'] > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png']

##### Identifying validation errors

These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:

- REF : The element is a recovered element. This element is referenced by another element in the project.
- SRE : T he symbol usage of the recovered element. Diagrams may have more than one recovered element displayed as a symbol. Each symbol of a recovered element is listed as a SRE error.
- URE : The element referencing the recovered element.

##### Handling recovered elements

It is recommended that you eliminate recovered elements from your model, using one of the following methods:

- Eliminate the causes of the element recovery . This is the case when recovered elements still exist. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.
- Restore recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.
- Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.

The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:

1525838641

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation' space='']

1574919148

INLINE

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *[ATTACHMENT filename='Project Decomposition Description.mdzip']*****sample model that uses the following projects: *[ATTACHMENT filename='Analysis and Design.mdzip'],**[ATTACHMENT filename='Code Level.mdzip'],* and***[ATTACHMENT filename='domain.mdzip'] .*

Alternatively, you can open the model from*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\project decomposition .*
