## PAGE 03366: (2026x) Mean Concept Relationships

- page_id: `272738244`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738244/2026x+Mean+Concept+Relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:16.572+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) Business Motivation Diagram > (2026x) Means Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487501502

1487501504

1487501504

The following table provides the relationships that exist between element types and their meaning.

| Relationship | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Makes Operative | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Mission lists every continuingActivity to ensure the Vision. |  |
| Component Of | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Strategy is the method orcourse of action that will be employed to achieve the Mission. |  |
| Implements | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Tactic realizes Strategies. |  |
| Enables | This link connects to a or to a . Meaning that a Strategy/Tactic makes another Strategy/Tactic doable. That is to say the latter Strategy/Tacticprovides an opportunity for the former Strategy/Tactic to be carried out. Use this linkto associate Strategy to another Strategy or Tactic to another Tactic. |  |
| Effects Enforcement Level | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Tactics influences the |  |
| Formulated Based On | This link connects or to a or . Meaningthat the Strategy or Tactic is planned according to what result the Business Policy orBusiness Rule desires. |  |
| Channel Efforts Towards | This link connects to a , or to an . Meaning that the Strategy iscoordinated as activities aimed towards Goals as Tactic towards Objective. For example, theTactic “Free delivery” channels efforts towards the Objective “5% increase in sales within 3months.” |  |
| Governs | This link connects or to a or . Meaning that the Business Policy or BusinessRule determines the Strategy or Tactic. |  |
| Basis For | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Business Policy is the key to achieve Business Rule. |  |
| Supports Achievement Of | This link connects or to a or . Meaning that the Business Policy provides anidea or reason to develop the Strategy or Tactic. |  |
| Acts As Regulation | This link connects a or a to External Influencer. |  |

1487501501

**Related element**

- Means Concepts

**Related diagrams**

- Business Motivation Diagram
- Process Definition Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736103 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03368: (2026x) Measurements

- page_id: `272736103`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736103/2026x+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:39.561+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .

Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the **Show Original Name of Measurement** symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Learn more about working with measurements:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .</p><p>Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the <strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Learn more about working with measurements:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c2b0623-cd0e-4255-b0a4-ebeb8a94756b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738299 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03369: (2026x) Measurements dialog

- page_id: `272738299`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738299/2026x+Measurements+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:56.572+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['measurements-dialog', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004957137

1004957139

1004957138

The **Measurements** dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] tables.

To open the **Measurements** dialog

- On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Measurements . The Measurements dialog opens.

The **Measurements** dialog consists of two tabs:

- Operational Exchange (or **Resource****Exchange** in the SV-6 table).
- Operational Exchange Item (or Resource ExchangeItem in the SV-6 table).

The**Operational Exchange** (or **Resource****Exchange**in the SV-6 and table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Exchanges in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The **Operational Exchange Item** (or**Resource ExchangeItem** in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the **Measurements** dialog are collected from the active table.

The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.

When you select the measurement and click the **OK** button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.

The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004957135

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80a03afc-594f-444d-bafc-5e9df3ab0cec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1004957137</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff73d16a-4d02-4fe6-b604-1c18bfbcf607"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1004957139</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d9a6674-efef-40c8-98de-53b5b305d0c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1004957138</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> tables.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show Columns</strong> button and select<strong> Measurements</strong>.<br />The <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog consists of two tabs:</p><ul><li><strong>Operational Exchange</strong> (or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Resource </strong><strong>Exchange</strong></span> in the SV-6 table).</li><li><strong>Operational Exchange Item</strong> (or <strong>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Item</strong> in the SV-6 table).</li></ul><p>The<strong> Operational Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange </strong></span>in the SV-6 and table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Exchanges in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The <strong>Operational Exchange Item</strong> (or<strong> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Item</strong> in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog are collected from the active table.</p><p>The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.</p><p>When you select the measurement and click the <strong>OK</strong> button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.</p><p>The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Measurements dialog" ac:alt="Measurements dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Measurements dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c20c3f4-ad7d-4b7b-932c-b5b0680b5711"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1004957135</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738473 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03371: (2026x) Measurements migration

- page_id: `272738473`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738473/2026x+Measurements+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:11.273+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > (2026x) Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.</p><p>The new approach includes:</p><ul><li>If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.</li><li>If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.</li><li>Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_migration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Measurements migration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.</h6><p>If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.</p>
````

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737941 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 03373: (2026x) Memory allocation

- page_id: `272737941`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737941/2026x+Memory+allocation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-25T13:47:23.054+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Low memory notification

1427463063

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Low memory notification

1427463065

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Low memory notification

1427463064

If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.

#### TIP: Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.

##### Recommended amount of allocated memory

The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.

##### Changing the amount of allocated memory

You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the Memory Settings category.
3. In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the Maximum Heap Size option. You should only change the value of the Thread Stack Size option if a *java.lang.StackOverflowError*error occurs.

#### TIP: Low memory notification

Low memory notification

While working on the project, you can get the *Low memory* notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click *Open Environment Options*to open the **Environment options** dialog where you can adjust memory settings.

To enable/disable notifications about low memory

1. Go to Environment Options > Display > Notify about low memory option

- 
  - Set to true to show notifications about low memory.
  - Set to false to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t his notification can be disabled, by clicking Do not show again option in the Low memory notification.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the modeling tool properties file.
2. In the **JAVA_ARGS** line, change the value of the **-Xmx** property. For example, change the **-Xmx800M** to **-Xmx1066M**.
3. Save and close the file.
4. Restart your modeling tool.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine

1. Go to Control Panel > System and Security > System > Advanced system settings. The System Properties dialog opens.
2. In the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables . The Environment Variables dialog opens.
3. In the System variables section, do one of the following:
  1. If the _JAVA_OPTIONS variable is not in the variables list, click New . New System Variable dialog opens.
  2. If the **_JAVA_OPTIONS** variable is in the variables list, select it and click **Edit**.**Edit System Variable**dialog opens.
4. Set **Variable name** to **_JAVA_OPTIONS** and **Variable value** to the desired memory amount, e.g. **-Xmx1066M**. Click **OK**. Changing options via the **Environment Variables** dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.

1427463062

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Background Task Manager' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Active validation period' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba5d00a9-bef0-475e-999a-12d56751127a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463063</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ca76881-7612-41c9-b6e9-e63076a33419"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463065</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b761f68-7235-4784-9d55-8dad2ef45222"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463064</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22dbf74-6683-4398-83b1-fc0c6753efad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory allocation concept</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Recommended amount of allocated memory</h3><p>The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.</p><h3>Changing the amount of allocated memory</h3><p>You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the <strong>Environment Variables</strong> dialog on your machine.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the amount of allocated memory in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Expand the <strong>General</strong> options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the <strong>Memory Settings</strong> category.</li><li data-uuid="5e6dd451-9a02-43a1-af99-5ead1b755655">In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the <strong>Maximum Heap Size </strong>option. You should only change the value of the <strong>Thread Stack Size </strong><span>option if a <em>java.lang.StackOverflowError </em>error occurs.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ba52b51-1396-48a9-872b-1e7e97404c59"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Low memory notification</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>While working on the project, you can get the <em>Low memory</em> notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click <em>Open Environment Options </em>to open the <strong>Environment options</strong> dialog where you can adjust memory settings.</p><p>To enable/disable notifications about low memory</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to<strong> Environment Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Display </strong>&gt;<strong> Notify about low memory option</strong></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Set to <em>true</em> to show notifications about low memory.</li><li><span>Set to </span><em>false</em><span> to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t</span>his notification can be disabled, by clicking <em>Do not show again</em><span> option </span>in the <strong>Low memory</strong> notification.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the modeling tool properties file.</li><li><p>In the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line, change the value of the <strong>-Xmx</strong> property. For example, change the <strong>-Xmx800M</strong> to <strong>-Xmx1066M</strong>.</p></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li>Restart your modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To change the amount of allocated memory in the <strong>Environment Variables</strong> dialog on your machine</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <strong>Control Panel</strong> &gt; <strong>System and Security </strong>&gt; <strong>System</strong> &gt; <strong>Advanced system settings. </strong>The<strong> System Properties </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Advanced</strong> tab, click <strong>Environment Variables</strong>. The <strong>Environment Variables </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>System variables</strong> section, do one of the following:<ol><li>If the <strong>_JAVA_OPTIONS</strong> variable is not in the variables list, click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">New</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">New System Variable</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If the <strong>_JAVA_OPTIONS</strong> variable is in the variables list, select it and click <strong>Edit</strong><span>. </span><strong>Edit System Variable</strong><span> dialog opens.</span><br /></span></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set <strong>Variable name</strong> to <strong>_JAVA_OPTIONS</strong> and <strong>Variable value</strong> to the desired memory amount, e.g. <strong>-Xmx1066M</strong>. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ebb2a11-1dd9-4e35-ac23-bbd208087f43"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Changing options via the <strong>Environment Variables</strong> dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2414574d-e565-492d-bb3e-b8c604af9e9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463062</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Improving performance" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Background Task Manager" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Memory monitor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Active validation period" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739419 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03374: (2026x) Memory and Disk Space Requirements for Large Models

- page_id: `272739419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739419/2026x+Memory+and+Disk+Space+Requirements+for+Large+Models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:21.593+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Guidelines for working with large models
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Memory

The memory allocation for the modeling tool by project size:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Allocated Memory 2 |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Earlier versions | 2021x and later 1 |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 7GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 11GB |

The amount of required memory proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

1the project elements count is different due to the profiling data storing metamodel optimized in 2021x (decrease by 40-80% in SysML v1 and UAF projects).

2these memory requirements are indicative and may vary by project specifics.

The operations that require more memory (1, 2, and 3.5 million of 2021x project elements respectively):

- UPDM migration to UAF - 10GB, 10GB, 36GB
- Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB
- Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB 3

3strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).

##### Disk Space

The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Disk Space |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 19.0 | 2021x |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 10GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 30GB |

The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

**Note:** Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.

Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

10GB, 10GB, 36GB</li><li>Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB</li><li>Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB <sup>3</sup></li></ul><p><sup>3 </sup>strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).</p><h3>Disk Space</h3><p>The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files: </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">Project Elements Count<br />(including used projects)</th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2">Disk Space</th></tr><tr><th style="text-align: center;">19.0</th><th style="text-align: center;">2021x</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">6GB</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;">2.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">2 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">10GB</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">3.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">30GB</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.</p><p><strong>Note:</strong> Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.</p><p>Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737946 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03375: (2026x) Memory monitor

- page_id: `272737946`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737946/2026x+Memory+monitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:25.489+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Running out of memory

1427409680

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Running out of memory

1427409682

238600894

**On this page:**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Running out of memory

1427409681

When working with larger models, turn on the Memory Monitor to monitor the memory used by a modeling tool. The Memory Monitor shows currently used memory and the current Java heap size. Typically, when you start working with a model, used memory increases because actions are stored in the undo list, and opened diagrams are loaded.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The memory monitor bar showing currently used memory and the current Java heap size.

##### Turning on the Memory Monitor

To turn on the Memory Monitor

- In the main menu of a modeling tool, select View > Status Line > Show Memory Monitor . The Memory Monitor bar appears in the bottom-right corner of the modeling tool window.

#### WARNING: Running out of memory

Running out of memory

When used memory takes more than 85% of the total heap size, the Memory Monitor bar turns red, indicating that a modeling tool may run out of memory soon.

##### Freeing unused memory with the Garbage Collector

If a modeling tool uses all the Java heap size, you can try to recover enough memory for saving a project with the Garbage Collector, but this may take some time.

To free unused memory with the Garbage Collector

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar and select Run Garbage Collector .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Memory Monitor pop-up window

If you find it impossible to use the modeling tool and/or see the Out of Memory error, you should dump the memory heap to the**.hprof**file that is created in the defined location on the computer and then submit this file to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC'] for further assistance regarding the memory consumption issue.

##### Dumping the memory heap

To dump the memory heap

Do one of the following:

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select Dump Memory Heap , and then set the desired name and location of the memory heap file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp.
- Press Ctrl+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory heap file.

You must create a new file for the heap dump since the memory heap cannot be dumped into an existing file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dumping memory heap

If the Memory Monitor bar is still red, save the model, restart the modeling tool, and reload the model. If the Memory Monitor bar stays red after reloading the model, you should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory allocation' space=''].

##### Dumping the memory histogram

To dump the memory histogram

Do one of the following:

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select Dump Memory Histogram , and then set the desired name and location of the memory histogram file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp.
- Press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory histogram file. To use the **Dump Memory Histogram** functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dumping memory histogram

1427409679

## PAGE 03376: (2026x) Message Boundary Event

- page_id: `272738368`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738368/2026x+Message+Boundary+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:01.981+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Boundary Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486349062

1486349065

1486349065

**Description**

A Message Boundary Event is triggered by an arrived message. Once triggered, it changes a normal flow into an exception flow or parallel.

A Message Boundary Event displays any of the following on the diagram:

- A Message Boundary Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation**

- Message Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_message.png']
- Message Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - False) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_message_cancelactivity.png']

1486349061

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) BPMNMessage' space='']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ef6acce-b959-4fbb-a6b2-300e407a2741"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486349062</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b72360ff-8b64-4205-9126-80559898a960"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486349065</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb36e8f8-fd88-4920-b250-be3710856ead"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486349065</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Message Boundary Event is triggered by an arrived message. Once triggered, it changes a normal flow into an exception flow or parallel.</p><p>A Message Boundary Event displays any of the following on the diagram:</p><ul><li>A Message Boundary Event name if the name is specified.</li><li>A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.</li><li>An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.</li></ul><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><ul><li>Message Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True)<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_event_message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Message Boundary Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </li><li>Message Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - False)<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_event_message_cancelactivity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Message Boundary Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f27a292-4b6b-4f6f-bb4c-31a1c50ea0c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486349061</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Boundary Events" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activities" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMNMessage" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMN Process Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMN Collaboration Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMN Choreography Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738358 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03377: (2026x) Message Catching Intermediate Event

- page_id: `272738358`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738358/2026x+Message+Catching+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:43.450+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Intermediate Catch Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Message Catching Intermediate Event is used to receive a message.

This event causes a process to continue if it is waiting for the message.

A Message Catching Intermediate Event displays any of the following on a diagram:

- A Message Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']
- Intermediate Catch Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 03378: (2026x) Message End Event

- page_id: `272738401`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738401/2026x+Message+End+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:11.647+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) End Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Message End Event indicates that a message will be sent when a process is completed.

This event displays any of the following on a diagram:

- A Message End Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- End Events
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738994 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03379: (2026x) Message Flow

- page_id: `272738994`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738994/2026x+Message+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:37.997+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486264927

1486264928

1486264927

Description

A Message Flow is used to show the flow of messages between two participants who are prepared to send and receive them.

- A Message Flow must connect separate Pools. It can be connected to the pool boundary or an element inside the pool.
- A Message Flow cannot connect two elements in the same Pool.

Messages that are sent by a Message Flow can be displayed on a diagram in two ways:

- overlapping the Message Flow
- associated with the Message Flow

However, they can also be hidden.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Message Flows between two pools.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Messages overlapping Message Flows

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Message Flows between Pools Inner elements

1486264925

**Related element**

- Pool and Lane

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737894 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03381: (2026x) Message Start Event

- page_id: `272737894`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737894/2026x+Message+Start+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:33.352+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Start Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Message Start Event means that a message from a participant has arrived and triggered the start of a process.

A Message Start Event displays any of the following on a diagram:

- A Message Start Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation**

- Interrupting Message Start Event [ATTACHMENT filename='start_event_interrupting.png']
- Non-interrupting Message Start Event [ATTACHMENT filename='start_event_nonint.png']

**Related elements**

- Start Events
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738828 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03385: (2026x) Method specification report

- page_id: `272738828`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738828/2026x+Method+specification+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:40.576+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Use Case driven
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the Method Specification report is to show the method design and its related use cases in detail to developers.

The report shows a class diagram and a list of all classes in the diagram. The list contains attributes and methods for each class. The report also shows the method specification, parameters, and algorithms.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The purpose of the Method Specification report is to show the method design and its related use cases in detail to developers.</p><p>The report shows a class diagram and a list of all classes in the diagram. The list contains attributes and methods for each class. The report also shows the method specification, parameters, and algorithms.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734972 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03386: (2026x) Metric Suites

- page_id: `272734972`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734972/2026x+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:41.127+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1497238846

1497238847

1895909674

**On this page**

4

1497238847

A metric is the item of information about the specific aspect of a model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of the model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help to track the evolution of model development.

800450

For better understanding of further material, get acquainted with the following concepts

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Metric Suite | A collection of parameter and metric definitions used to calculate the metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model. |
| Parameter definition | The variable that is used to calculate metrics. It can be an element type (including both relationship and DSL element types), particular element, data type, or data type value. |
| Metric definition | The attribute of a Metric Suite defining the formula for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |
| Validation-based metric definition | The attribute of a Metric Suite specifying the validation rule for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |

In general, a Metric Suite is a Class with the «MetricSuite» stereotype applied. Both parameter and metric definitions are attributes of a Metric Suite.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

One metric is the result of a single calculation of a metric definition. The metrics of all the metric definitions of a Metric Suite together with relevant parameters are stored in a single instance of a Metric Suite (as slot values). To analyse and manage instances of a Metric Suite with a modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metric table' space=''].

##### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
Creating Metric Suites

There are several conventions that you must know about Metric Suites before creating one:

- Metric Suite must have a target. A target specifies model elements, for which metrics of the particular metric suite can be calculated. The target value can be any type of elements or even a particular element of your model.
- Metric Suite must have one parameter definition declared as context. The element, which is selected for calculating metrics, is automatically set as the value of the context parameter definition.
- Metric Suite must have a date tracker, so that you could see the timestamp of each metric.

To create and define a Metric Suite, complete the following steps:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a new Metric Suite' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space=''] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying parameter definitions' space=''] (including the context parameter definition).
4. Build [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Building metric definitions' space=''] and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Building validation-based metric definitions' space=''] .

It’s recommended to have a single person per project, who is responsible for creating and managing all the Metric Suites of the project.

1497238846

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specific use cases' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733677 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03387: (2026x) Metric table

- page_id: `272733677`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733677/2026x+Metric+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:45.384+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

222736122

222736125

222736121

**On this page**

222736124

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Architect and Enterprise editions only.

The Metric Table is one of four tables. The Metric Table allows you to collect various metrics used to evaluate the current state of your model. A Metric is an item of information about a specific aspect of the model. Each row in the table represents a set of metrics calculated according to the metric suites specified by the user.

To understand the material better, you need to be familiar with the following concepts.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Metric suite | A collection of parameter and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model. |
| Parameter definition | A variable used for calculating metrics. It can be an element type (including both relationship and DSL element types), a particular element, data type, or data type value. |
| Metric definition | A formula for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |
| Validation-based metric definition | The attribute of a Metric Suite specifying the validation rule for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |

Actually, a metric suite is a class with the *«MetricSuite»* stereotype applied. Both parameter and metric definitions are attributes of the metric suite.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Purpose**

Calculated at regular intervals, metrics help track the evolution of the model development. One metric is the result of a single calculation of a metric definition. The metrics of all the metric definitions of the metric suite, together with relevant parameters, are stored in a single instance of the metric suite as slot values. Use the Metric Table to analyze and manage the instances of the metric suite.

With a help of a Metric Table you can:

- Calculate model metrics.
- Change the parameters of the metrics and recalculate them.
- Customize the representation of the metrics.
- Export the data to an .html , .xlsx , or .csv file.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Specific tasks in the Metric Table**

The following links outline the specific procedures when working with the Metric Table:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730964 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03388: (2026x) Metrics

- page_id: `272730964`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730964/2026x+Metrics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:50.779+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management > (2026x) Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A metric is information about a specific aspect of the model. A collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. When calculated at regular intervals, they can help track the evolution of the model's development.

There are two metric suites dedicated to coverage analysis:

- The Design metric suite checks incoming satisfy relations.
- The Test cases metric suite checks incoming verify relations.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of metrics table.

When working with requirements, it is useful to know what and how many requirements are covered by design elements (satisfy relations), by test cases (verify relations), are user requirements covered with system requirements, and how many requirements are already implemented or tested. In these situations metrics help you evaluate the current state of requirements in your model and track the progress of their coverage.

The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element) is considered as covered if it satisfies one of the following conditions:

- A requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element.
- The owning requirement is covered.
- All owned requirements are covered.

The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement) is considered as covered if the requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element:

- If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relations, grouping requirements are not considered as satisfied unless they also have incoming satisfy relations.
- If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not considered as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Examples of covered requirements.

To use coverage analysis metrics

1. Select the package that includes the requirements.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Metrics > New Metric Table .
3. In the Create Metric Table dialog, set to true one of the metric suite:
  - Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element).
  - Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement).
4. Click OK . The new metric table with calculated results is created.

You can create custom metric suites using structured expressions or scripts. For detailed procedures on creating metric suites, calculating metrics, and managing metric results, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metric Suites' space='']Creating Metric Suites and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metric table' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A metric is information about a specific aspect of the model. A collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. When calculated at regular intervals, they can help track the evolution of the model's development.</p><p>There are two metric suites dedicated to coverage analysis:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4dce7850-45d7-4aa9-a3b7-a4643916e0f3">The Design metric suite checks incoming satisfy relations.</li><li data-uuid="f3cc3224-3b7d-42c4-a1db-383c756130e1">The Test cases metric suite checks incoming verify relations.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metrics_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Metrics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Example of metrics table.</h6><p>When working with requirements, it is useful to know what and how many requirements are covered by design elements (satisfy relations), by test cases (verify relations), are user requirements covered with system requirements, and how many requirements are already implemented or tested. In these situations metrics help you evaluate the current state of requirements in your model and track the progress of their coverage.</p><p>The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element) is considered as covered if it satisfies one of the following conditions:</p><ul><li data-uuid="14a3fbaa-23bc-4bcb-b1ce-90cf5890a5ae">A requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element.</li><li data-uuid="85e7fe5e-f7b3-4590-a4fb-f5061ff96496">The owning requirement is covered.</li><li data-uuid="154ef613-11b5-47bc-b4cd-a912747a5a8d">All owned requirements are covered.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement) is considered as covered if the requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e3d5bb46-3f75-4f59-b4c0-a8d27869e4d5">If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relations, grouping requirements are not considered as satisfied unless they also have incoming satisfy relations.</li><li data-uuid="05bb3d53-dae7-42a3-895e-d14493299637">If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not considered as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="examples_of_covered_requirements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Metrics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Examples of covered requirements.</h6><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To use coverage analysis metrics</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="70bc75db-8f5c-47cc-8542-757a8a5eb3d1">Select the package that includes the requirements.</li><li data-uuid="a7d94ab0-fcbe-403e-90f8-76af3678af11">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New </strong><strong>Metric</strong><strong> Table</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="4cefb6e4-ba80-40e9-85d5-3fa0d7dc17ac">In the <strong>Create</strong><strong> Metric</strong><strong> Table </strong>dialog, set to <em>true</em> one of the metric suite:<br /><ul><li>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element).</li><li>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement).</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b5b9e253-31a8-4897-b7bc-dfb4ef7f6101">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The new metric table with calculated results is created.</li></ol><p>You can create custom metric suites using structured expressions or scripts. For detailed procedures on creating metric suites, calculating metrics, and managing metric results, please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Metric Suites" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Creating Metric</span> Suites</ac:link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Metric table" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"><span>.</span></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731019 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03389: (2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)

- page_id: `272731019`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731019/2026x+Microsoft+Office+Excel+worksheet+XLSX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:58.481+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix B: office open XML format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Multi-Line Statements in *XLSX*

All multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.

The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single cell. The figures below show samples of invalid usage of the **#if** and **#foreach** statements between cells respectively.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Executing multi-line statements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid usage of #foreach statements.

In the first figure, since the body of the #if statement ($e.name) resides in the A2 cell, not in the A1 cell, the body will not be generated when generating a report, regardless of the evaluation of the #if statement. The code shown in the second figure will break the structure of a spreadsheet document.

The following two figures demonstrate samples of valid usage of the #if and #foreach statements respectively.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid usage of an #if statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid usage of a #foreach statement.

- A VTL Macro must be declared within a single cell. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single cell, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid usage of a VTL Macro.

The macro will copy all contents between #macro and #end. Cells and rows will be included in the macro as well. Once this record has been inserted, the macro content will break the document structure.

The following figure demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #macro statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid usage of a VTL Macro.

#### Creating data for multiple rows

The **#foreach** directive can only be used in a single cell record. To create data for multiple rows, use the **#forrow** directive instead (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The forrow directive.

The figure below demonstrates the output of the above code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from figure 7.

#### Creating data for multiple columns

**#forcol** is used for creating data for multiple columns (see the figure below). This statement can be used in conjunction with the **#forrow** statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The forcol directive.

The following figure demonstrates the output of the above code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Displaying content in a cell

Texts in any generated report are always wrapped. Also, the cells’ width in the generated report depends on the cells’ width in the report template used. For example, an *XLSX* report template in the first figure below will generate an output report as shown in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The forrow directive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Limitation when used in Microsoft Office Excel worksheet

You cannot use **#sectionBegin** and **#includeSection** in *XLSX*. If you try to use **#sectionBegin** or **#includeSection** in an *XLSX* report template (the first figure below) an error message will open (the second figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid usage of a sectionBegin directive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The error message from running the code in the figure above.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The figures below show samples of invalid usage of the <strong>#if</strong> and <strong>#foreach</strong> statements between cells respectively.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_6.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Executing multi-line statements.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_7.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid usage of #foreach statements.</h6><p>In the first figure, since the body of the #if statement ($e.name) resides in the A2 cell, not in the A1 cell, the body will not be generated when generating a report, regardless of the evaluation of the #if statement. The code shown in the second figure will break the structure of a spreadsheet document.</p><p style="text-align: left;">The following two figures demonstrate samples of valid usage of the #if and #foreach statements respectively.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_8.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Valid usage of an #if statement.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_9.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Valid usage of a #foreach statement.</h6><ul><li>A VTL Macro must be declared within a single cell. To create data for multiple rows, use the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive instead (see the following figure).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_12.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The forrow directive.</h6><p style="text-align: left;">The figure below demonstrates the output of the above code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_13.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from figure 7.</h6><h2>Creating data for multiple columns</h2><p><strong>#forcol</strong> is used for creating data for multiple columns (see the figure below). This statement can be used in conjunction with the <strong>#forrow</strong> statement.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_14.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The forcol directive.</h6><p>The following figure demonstrates the output of the above code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_15.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from the figure above.</h6><h2>Displaying content in a cell</h2><p>Texts in any generated report are always wrapped. Also, the cells’ width in the generated report depends on the cells’ width in the report template used. For example, an <em>XLSX</em> report template in the first figure below will generate an output report as shown in the second figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Wrapped Text (Column B) in an XLSX Report Template" ac:alt="Sample of Wrapped Text (Column B) in an XLSX Report Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_16.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The forrow directive.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Wrapping Text Output in XLSX" ac:alt="Wrapping Text Output in XLSX"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_17.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from the figure above.</h6><h2>Limitation when used in Microsoft Office Excel worksheet</h2><p>You cannot use <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#includeSection</strong> in <em>XLSX</em>. If you try to use <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> or <strong>#includeSection</strong> in an <em>XLSX</em> report template (the first figure below) an error message will open (the second figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_18.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid usage of a sectionBegin directive.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 14- The error message from running the code in figure 13..png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The error message from running the code in the figure above.</h6>
