<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730970 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03390: (2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)

- page_id: `272730970`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730970/2026x+Microsoft+Office+PowerPoint+presentation+PPTX
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:59.421+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix B: office open XML format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A presentation document requires a special document template. This template does not contain any content order, and all text content is always placed inside a text box. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change a Text box position. You can also place a Text box in the same positions as others (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of PPTX template.

#### Multi-line statements in PPTX

Similar to *XLSX*, all multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.

1. The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single text box. The following code shows a sample of invalid usage of the#foreach statement between the text boxes. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Since the PPTX template does not provide the statement order, the template will not be interpreted as the order of the displayed images (text boxes). For example, $uc.name may not be processed after #foreach($uc in $UseCase) has been completely processed. The following code demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #foreach statement. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. A VTL Macro must be declared within a single text box. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single text box. See the following code example. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Since each text box does not have any sequence order, the macro cannot record any content between the text boxes. The following code example demonstrates a valid usage of the #macro statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Creating data for multiple slides

In PPTX report templates, you can use the #forpage directive to create additional slide(s) in your presentation. You can use #forpage and #endpage directives in any text box. However, the #endpage directive must be on same slide as the #forpage directive or on the following slide, but not before the slide which contains the #forpage directive. All directives on the slide(s) between 1) the slide the #forpage directive appears and 2) the slide the #endpage directive appears will be included within the #forpage statement. For example, the template in the first figure below will produce the output as shown in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating data for multiple pages using the forpage directive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Creating a page with conditions

Since a directive in the *PPTX* report template does not provide any statement order (unless residing in the same text box), you cannot used the #if directive together with the #forpage directive. See figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The code above will not produce the output report as expected, because the #forpage directive automatically covers all directives in the current page regardless of the statement order. Consequently, the #if directive may not be interpreted after the #forpage directive.

To avoid this problem, you can use the $report.filterElement($elements, $types) method. This helper method provides the element filter for the specified type. In this case, use the following code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more details on $report.filterElement($elements, $types), see Section *[Helper Modules](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Helper+tools)*.

#### Limitation when used in Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation

**#sectionBegin**, **#includeSection** cannot be used in any *PPTX* report template. If you try, for example, to use **#sectionBegin** in a *PPTX* report template, an error message (the figure below) will open.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The error message dialog that appears from running an invalid sectionBegin directive.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A presentation document requires a special document template. This template does not contain any content order, and all text content is always placed inside a text box. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change a Text box position. You can also place a Text box in the same positions as others (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of PPTX Template" ac:alt="Sample of PPTX Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_20.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">A sample of PPTX template.</h6><h2>Multi-line statements in PPTX</h2><p>Similar to <em>XLSX</em>, all multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.</p><ol><li><p>The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single text box. The following code shows a sample of invalid usage of the#foreach statement between the text boxes.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_21.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adb06cf4-2d0c-48ff-a8d3-82434924a373"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Since the PPTX template does not provide the statement order, the template will not be interpreted as the order of the displayed images (text boxes). For example, $uc.name may not be processed after #foreach($uc in $UseCase) has been completely processed. The following code demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #foreach statement.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_22.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>A VTL Macro must be declared within a single text box. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single text box. See the following code example.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_23.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>Since each text box does not have any sequence order, the macro cannot record any content between the text boxes. The following code example demonstrates a valid usage of the #macro statement.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_24.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Creating data for multiple slides</h2><p>In PPTX report templates, you can use the #forpage directive to create additional slide(s) in your presentation. You can use #forpage and #endpage directives in any text box. However, the #endpage directive must be on same slide as the #forpage directive or on the following slide, but not before the slide which contains the #forpage directive. All directives on the slide(s) between 1) the slide the #forpage directive appears and 2) the slide the #endpage directive appears will be included within the #forpage statement. For example, the template in the first figure below will produce the output as shown in the second figure below.<br /> </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_25.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Creating data for multiple pages using the forpage directive.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_26.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from the figure above.</h6><h2>Creating a page with conditions</h2><p>Since a directive in the <em>PPTX</em> report template does not provide any statement order (unless residing in the same text box), you cannot used the #if directive together with the #forpage directive. See figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Looping With Condition in General Style" ac:alt="Sample of Looping With Condition in General Style"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_27.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The code above will not produce the output report as expected, because the #forpage directive automatically covers all directives in the current page regardless of the statement order. Consequently, the #if directive may not be interpreted after the #forpage directive.</p><p>To avoid this problem, you can use the $report.filterElement($elements, $types) method. This helper method provides the element filter for the specified type. In this case, use the following code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Looping With Conditional Filter" ac:alt="Sample of Looping With Conditional Filter"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_28.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>For more details on $report.filterElement($elements, $types), see Section <em><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Helper+tools">Helper Modules</a></em>.</p><h2>Limitation when used in Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation</h2><p><strong>#sectionBegin</strong>, <strong>#includeSection</strong> cannot be used in any <em>PPTX</em> report template. If you try, for example, to use <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> in a <em>PPTX</em> report template, an error message (the figure below) will open.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_29.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"> </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4- The error message dialog that appears from running an invalid sectionBegin directive..png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The error message dialog that appears from running an invalid sectionBegin directive.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731042 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03391: (2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)

- page_id: `272731042`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731042/2026x+Microsoft+Office+Word+document+DOCX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:01.559+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix B: office open XML format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports most *DOCX* features. You can place the *VTL* codes inside core (properties) and content of any *DOCX* file. All syntax usable in *RTF* can also be used in *DOCX*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document.

#### Limitations when used in Microsoft Office Word document

You cannot use multi-line statements in different objects. If you try to use them in*DOCX*, an error message will open. See the following example.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Error Message of Invalid Usage of the Multi-line Statements in DOCX.

#### Creating data for multiple columns

**#forcol** is used for creating data for multiple columns in a row. This statement must be defined in table and can be used in conjunction with the **#forrow** statement. For example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Sample elements in the Containment tree.

**Template Code:**

| Name | #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**#forcol** may create different number of columns in each row. See the example below.

**Template Code:**

| #forrow($p in $sPackage) $p.name | #forcol($o in $sorter.sort($p.ownedElement))$o.name#endcol#endrow |
| --- | --- |

**Output:**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**#forcol** must be defined as the first statement in a column because it is parsed and processed before other statements in the column except **#forrow**. See the example below.

**Template Code**

| Name | #set($p = $Package) #forcol($ap in $p)$ap.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**Above code will be parsed to**

| Name | #forcol($ap in $p) #set($p = $Package)$ap.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

So, the template code should be:

Template Code:

| Name#set($p = $Package) | #forcol($ap in $p) $ap.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**#forcol** will create dynamic columns in a row. This directive does not create columns for the whole table. See the example below.

**Template Code:**

| Name | #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Docx supports at most 63 columns per row (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Docx supports at most 63 columns per row.

#### Creating merged column horizontally for DOCX

ReportWizard provides utility functions for creating table properties. The functions of this module are accessible from templates through **$tableprop**. In this version, ReportWizard provides only **$tableprop.mergeColumns()** to create merged columns horizontally in *DOCX* template.

This statement must be defined in table.

****

**1. $tableprop.mergeColumns(int number)**

Merging columns in specified number

Where the parameter is:

- number - the number of columns to merge. This value has to be of type integer and start from 2.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($Package.size())Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

**2. $tableprop.mergeColumns(String stringNumber)**

Merging columns in specified number as String

Where the parameter is:

- stringNumber - the string value of number of columns to be merged. This value has to be of type integer string and start from “2”.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns(“3”) Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**$table.mergeColumns()** must be defined as the first statement in the column because it is parsed and processed before other statements in column expect **#forrow** and **#forcol.** See the example below.

****

**Template Code:**

| Project | #set($gValue = $Package.size() +1)$tableprop.mergeColumns($gValue)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package) $p.name#endcol | No Package |

Above code will be parsed to

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($gValue)#set($gValue = $Package.size() + 1)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package) $p.name#endcol | No Package |

**Output:**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

So, the template code should be:

**Template Code:**

| Project#set($gValue = $Package.size() + 1) | $tableprop.mergeColumns($gValue)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol | No Package |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**$tableprop.mergecolumns()** will make a column to be a merged column, it does not change the number of columns in a row. See the following example.

****

**Template Code:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns(“5”)Packages |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |  |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The number of columns in the first row is still 3.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports most <em>DOCX</em> features. You can place the <em>VTL</em> codes inside core (properties) and content of any <em>DOCX</em> file. All syntax usable in <em>RTF</em> can also be used in <em>DOCX</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document" ac:alt="A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_1.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document.</h6><h2>Limitations when used in Microsoft Office Word document</h2><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You cannot use multi-line statements in different objects. If you try to use them in </span><em>DOCX</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, an error message will open. See the following example.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Invalid Usage of Multi-line Statement in DOCX" ac:alt="Invalid Usage of Multi-line Statement in DOCX"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_4.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_export.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Error Message of Invalid Usage of the Multi-line Statements in DOCX.</h6><h2>Creating data for multiple columns</h2><p><strong>#forcol</strong> is used for creating data for multiple columns in a row. This statement must be defined in table and can be used in conjunction with the <strong>#forrow</strong> statement. For example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Sample elements in MagicDraw Containment Tree" ac:alt="Sample elements in MagicDraw Containment Tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Sample elements in the Containment tree.</h6><p><strong>Template Code:</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><pre>Name</pre></td><td><pre> #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b228f395-e33f-4df1-891f-3c06c3e58988"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>#forcol</strong> may create different number of columns in each row. See the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Template Code:</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>#forrow($<span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);">p</span> in $sPackage) $p.name</td><td>#forcol($o in $sorter.sort($p.ownedElement))<br />$o.name#endcol#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66ec4a0d-2a3a-4106-af12-75e51f6dc928"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>#forcol</strong> must be defined as the first statement in a column because it is parsed and processed before other statements in the column except <strong>#forrow</strong>. See the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Template Code</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>Name</td><td>#set($p = $Package) #forcol($ap in $p)<br />$ap.name#endcol</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Above code will be parsed to </strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>Name</td><td>#forcol($ap in $p) #set($p = $Package)<br />$ap.name#endcol</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>So, the template code should be:</p><p>Template Code:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>Name#set($p = $Package)</td><td>#forcol($ap in $p) $ap.name#endcol</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40a9cd37-ed82-4ed0-b604-1844c3293d67"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>#forcol</strong> will create dynamic columns in a row. This directive does not create columns for the whole table. See the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Template Code:</strong></p><table><tbody><tr><td><pre>Name</pre></td><td><pre> #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol</pre></td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ef7bfbe-b319-46e0-ae7d-49e2de9677a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Docx supports at most 63 columns per row (see the following figure).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Docx supports at most 63 columns per row" ac:alt="Docx supports at most 63 columns per row"><ri:attachment ri:filename="section-26-1-2-diagram-five.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Docx supports at most 63 columns per row.</h6><h2>Creating merged column horizontally for DOCX</h2><p>ReportWizard provides utility functions for creating table properties. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729722 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03392: (2026x) Migrating from BPMN 1.1

- page_id: `272729722`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729722/2026x+Migrating+from+BPMN+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:36.229+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > (2026x) Migration
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The MagicDraw 16.8 and earlier versions support BPMN 1.1 and Business Process diagrams, this let you to model business processes using the BPMN 1.1 notation.

The support for BPMN 1.1 and Business Process diagrams is no longer available from MagicDraw version 17.0.1. It is replaced by the Cameo Business Modeler support for the BPMN 2.0 specification.

You can convert your MagicDraw projects containing old BPMN profile and Business Process diagrams to the new BPMN 2.0 standard. A backup file will be created for the project conversion. This backup file lets you find your BPMN 1.1 models when you need them.

To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by opening it

1. Open a MagicDraw project that contains a BPMN Profile or Business Process diagram. A Question dialog opens.
2. Click Yes to convert the project.
3. Click OK .

To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by importing it

1. On the main menu, click File > Import From > BPMN 1.1 Project . The Open BPMN 1.1 Project dialog opens.
2. Select a project that contains a Business Process diagram and click Open .
3. Click OK .

A backup file project_name_bpmn1.1.bak is created when the project has been migrated.The file includes the BPMN 1.1 project before the migration.

To convert a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0

Before migrating a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0, make sure that other users have not locked the BPMN elements.

1. On the main menu, click File > Import From > BPMN 1.1 Project . A Question dialog opens.
2. Click Yes to convert the project. Another Question dialog opens to inform you that all elements will be locked.
3. Click Yes .

A backup file for a Teamwork project will not be created when migrating the project to BPMN 2.0. Use a Teamwork version as a backup copy that has included the BPMN 1.1 Business Process diagrams before migrating the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It is replaced by the Cameo Business Modeler support for the BPMN 2.0 specification.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can convert your MagicDraw projects containing old BPMN profile and Business Process diagrams to the new BPMN 2.0 standard. A backup file will be created for the project conversion. This backup file lets you find your BPMN 1.1 models when you need them.</p><p><br /></p><p>To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by opening it</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a MagicDraw project that contains a BPMN Profile or Business Process diagram. A Question dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to convert the project.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by importing it</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>BPMN 1.1 Project</strong>. The <strong>Open BPMN 1.1 Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select a project that contains a Business Process diagram and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d199a91a-85a8-45af-bef3-64c4e7aedeca"><ac:rich-text-body>A backup file project_name_bpmn1.1.bak is created when the project has been migrated.The file includes the BPMN 1.1 project before the migration.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To convert a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82d243a8-680b-468b-b2e1-03a123601b91"><ac:rich-text-body>Before migrating a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0, make sure that other users have not locked the BPMN elements.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>BPMN 1.1 Project</strong>. A Question dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to convert the project. Another Question dialog opens to inform you that all elements will be locked.</li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de4d1ae3-6489-4e7f-b09a-c8c0c330ba39"><ac:rich-text-body>A backup file for a Teamwork project will not be created when migrating the project to BPMN 2.0. Use a Teamwork version as a backup copy that has included the BPMN 1.1 Business Process diagrams before migrating the project.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738756 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03393: (2026x) Migrating Server Projects

- page_id: `272738756`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738756/2026x+Migrating+Server+Projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-29T08:45:43.314+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.

To migrate a server project

1. In the Model Browser, open the **Locked Elements** tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users. If elements are locked by other users, each user must unlock their own locked elements.Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.
2. Perform the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DoDAF Projects Migration' space=''] .
3. Commit the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.</p><p><br /></p><p>To migrate a server project</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Locked Elements</strong> tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db086d4-c5c7-4f6c-8fcb-68f3472a4ca6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If elements are locked by other users, each user must unlock their own locked elements.</p><p>Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Perform the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DoDAF Projects Migration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project migration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Commit the project.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729701 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03394: (2026x) Migrating to UML Profile for BPMN2 v1.0

- page_id: `272729701`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729701/2026x+Migrating+to+UML+Profile+for+BPMN2+v1.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:33.900+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > (2026x) Migration
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The BPMN2 is supported by Cameo Business Modeler Plugin 17.0 and later versions of the tool; however, some further changes have been made in Cameo Business Modeler Plugin 19.0 to improve the UML Profile for BPMN2 v1.0 support. The migration comes with the changes listed below:

- Some BPMN elements have been renamed

| Old Name | New Name |
| --- | --- |
| Message | BPMNMessage |
| Relationship | BPMNRelationship |
| BPMNImport | Import |
| Operation | BPMNOperation |
| Association | BPMNAssociation |
| EventGatewayType | EventBasedGatewayType |
| Event | BPMNEvent |

- Some Stereotypes' metaclasses have been changed

| Stereotype | Old Metaclass Name | New Metaclass Name |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Resource | Actor, Class | Class |
| PartnerRole | Actor, Class | Class |
| ReceiveTask | OpaqueAction | AcceptEventAction |
| SendTask | OpaqueAction | CallOperationAction |
| ServiceTask | OpaqueAction | CallOperationAction |
| Task | OpaqueAction | Action |
| BPMNMessage | CentralBufferNode | Class |
| Message Flow | Dependency | Information Flow |

Please check your model if you use Stereotypes for BPMN elements. They could have been affected by the migration.

- Some elements have been removed from the BPMN Profile

| Removed Element |
| --- |
| MessageFlowNode |
| Correlation |
| AttributeProperty |
| PinProperty |
| VariableProperty |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">The BPMN2 is supported by Cameo Business Modeler Plugin 17.0 and later versions of the tool; however, some further changes have been made in Cameo Business Modeler Plugin 19.0 to improve the UML Profile for BPMN2 v1.0 support. The migration comes with the changes listed below: </p><ul><li>Some BPMN elements have been renamed</li></ul><table class="relative-table" style="width: 23.1487%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 48.9311%;" /><col style="width: 51.0689%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Old Name</th><th>New Name</th></tr><tr><td>Message</td><td>BPMNMessage</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Relationship</td><td colspan="1">BPMNRelationship</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">BPMNImport</td><td colspan="1">Import</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operation</td><td colspan="1">BPMNOperation</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Association</td><td colspan="1">BPMNAssociation</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">EventGatewayType</td><td colspan="1">EventBasedGatewayType</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Event</td><td colspan="1">BPMNEvent</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ul><li>Some Stereotypes' metaclasses have been changed</li></ul><table class="relative-table" style="width: 27.6467%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 30.4175%;" /><col style="width: 33.996%;" /><col style="width: 35.5865%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Stereotype</th><th>Old Metaclass Name</th><th colspan="1">New Metaclass Name</th></tr><tr><td>Resource</td><td>Actor, Class</td><td colspan="1">Class</td></tr><tr><td>PartnerRole</td><td>Actor, Class</td><td colspan="1">Class</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ReceiveTask</td><td colspan="1">OpaqueAction</td><td colspan="1">AcceptEventAction</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SendTask</td><td colspan="1">OpaqueAction</td><td colspan="1">CallOperationAction</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ServiceTask</td><td colspan="1">OpaqueAction</td><td colspan="1">CallOperationAction</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Task</td><td colspan="1">OpaqueAction</td><td colspan="1">Action</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">BPMNMessage</td><td colspan="1">CentralBufferNode</td><td colspan="1">Class</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Message Flow</td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">Information Flow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43179ce2-b127-453c-aae1-d8b16115074d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check your model if you use Stereotypes for BPMN elements. They could have been affected by the migration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>Some elements have been removed from the BPMN Profile</li></ul><table class="relative-table" style="width: 262.0px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 0.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Removed Element</th></tr><tr><td>MessageFlowNode</td></tr><tr><td>Correlation</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">AttributeProperty</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">PinProperty</td></tr><tr><td>VariableProperty</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738791 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 03398: (2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3

- page_id: `272738791`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738791/2026x+Migration+from+UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.3
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-12T12:10:32.089+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) UAF projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is **NO DIRECT MIGRATION**from **UAF 1.0** (last supported modeling tool version - **19.0**) to **UAF 1.3** (first supported modeling tool version - **2026x**).

##### How to migrate

To be able to migrate your **UAF 1.0** project, you need to perform the following steps:​

1. Open your UAF 1.0 project with the modeling tool 2022x or 2024x version, and migrate it to UAF 1.2 . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2' space='']>>]]>
2. Open the **UAF 1.2** project with the modeling tool **2026x** version and migrate it to **UAF 1.3**. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3' space='']>>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">There is <strong>NO DIRECT MIGRATION </strong>from <strong>UAF 1.0</strong> (last supported modeling tool version - <strong>19.0</strong>) to <strong>UAF 1.3</strong> (first supported modeling tool version - <strong>2026x</strong>).</span></p><h3>How to migrate</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span>To be able to migrate your <strong>UAF 1.0</strong> project, you need to perform the following steps:</span><span>​</span></p><ol><li data-uuid="cc547270-adf1-43ea-9efd-717c27ffe713">Open your <strong>UAF 1.0</strong> project with the modeling tool <strong>2022x</strong> or <strong>2024x</strong> version, and migrate it to <strong>UAF 1.2</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9d6f2b78-d6ca-4385-94e6-36cec2d681d6"><span>Open the <strong>UAF 1.2</strong> project with the modeling tool <strong>2026x</strong> version and migrate it to <strong>UAF 1.3</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ol>
````

## PAGE 03399: (2026x) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2

- page_id: `272733394`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733394/2026x+Migration+from+UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the migration to UAF 1.2, the following profile changes takes place:

The duration of the projects migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2 depends on the project sizes. In the following chart, you can see the duration of local and Teamwork Cloud projects migration:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For the recommendations of RAM for the projects migration, see [Installation, licensing, and system requirements](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&version=2026x).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733665 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03403: (2026x) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1

- page_id: `272733665`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733665/2026x+Migration+from+UPDM+3+UAF+1.0+beta+to+UAF+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:43.065+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) UAF projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:

- 
- 

##### Migrating to UAF 1.0

To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0

1. Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.
2. Save the project.

For more information about changes, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0' space='']

##### Migrating to UAF 1.1

After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.0"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[First, you need to migrate your project to UAF 1.0. ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Then you can migrate that project to UAF 1.1.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.0</h3><p>To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6acb6dfe-1750-475f-bab8-7fe3ff6c30d2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Save the project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information about changes, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0" /></ac:link></p><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.1</h3><p>After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1" /></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730055 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03405: (2026x) MinExclusive

- page_id: `272730055`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730055/2026x+MinExclusive
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:31.256+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) XML schemas > (2026x) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDminExclusive. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: minExclusive XML representation summary

minExclusive XML representation summary

```text
<minExclusive
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = anySimpleType
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</minExclusive>
```

```text
{value} must be in the value space of {base type definition}.
```

#### PANEL: minExclusive XML code sample

minExclusive XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which limits values to integers greater than or equal to 100, using minExclusive.
```

```text
<simpleType name= 'more- than-ninety-nine'>
```

```text
<restriction base= 'integer'>		<minExclusive value= '99'/>	</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType>
```

```text
Note that the value space of this datatype is identical to the previous one (named 'one-­hundred-or-more').
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDminExclusive. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.</p><ul><li>value – to Attribute initial value.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13a246b5-bcac-4403-9805-e8e403e1da8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">minExclusive XML representation summary</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;minExclusive</pre><pre>	fixed = boolean : false</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	value = anySimpleType</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt; </pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?)</pre><pre>&lt;/minExclusive&gt;</pre><pre>{value} must be in the value space of {base type definition}.</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20e78d56-b4c3-431c-879c-f31c5d2b56c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">minExclusive XML code sample</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which limits values to integers greater than or equal to 100, using minExclusive.</pre><pre>&lt;simpleType name= 'more- than-ninety-nine'&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;restriction base= 'integer'&gt;<br />		&lt;minExclusive value= '99'/&gt;<br />	&lt;/restriction&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/simpleType&gt;</pre><pre>Note that the value space of this datatype is identical to the previous one (named 'one-­hundred-or-more').</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which requires strings to have at least one character (i.e., the empty string is not in the value space of this datatype).

<simpleType name='non-empty-string'›
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738480 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03408: (2026x) Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps

- page_id: `272738480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738480/2026x+Missing+data+in+diagrams+matrices+tables+and+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:13.033+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > (2026x) Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Since UPDM 2 profile [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removed elements' space=''], the Element Type of matrices, tables, and relation maps becomes empty after the migration. The same goes for the customized elements derived from the removed UPDM2 elements. If the UPDM2 [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Replaced and renamed elements' space=''] to the UAF element, the UAF element is set as an Element Type (criteria).

#### NOTE: Exception

Exception

If Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, Service Interaction was set as an Element Type criteria, after the migration an Interaction replaces those elements.

After the migration from UPDM 2 to UAF, some tags are no longer supported. During the migration process, all the information from those tags is moved to the**To Do**property of the tag owner. To find this property, open the appropriate element's Specification window. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Since UPDM 2 profile <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Removed elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elements were removed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the Element Type of matrices, tables, and relation maps becomes empty after the migration. The same goes for the customized elements derived from the removed UPDM2 elements. If the UPDM2 <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Replaced and renamed elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element is migrated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the UAF element, the UAF element is set as an Element Type (criteria).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f830871-f7d3-43f9-a110-feeded1958ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Exception</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If Operational Event Trace, <span>Resource Event Trace, <span>Service Interaction was set as an Element Type criteria, after the migration an Interaction replaces those elements.</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After the migration from UPDM 2 to UAF, some tags are no longer supported. During the migration process, all the information from those tags is moved to the </span><strong>To Do</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> property of the tag owner. To find this property, open the appropriate element's Specification window. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_tagged_values.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3>Removed tag added to ToDo property</h3><p>Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730005 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03410: (2026x) Missing expression language

- page_id: `272730005`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730005/2026x+Missing+expression+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:27.072+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Macro engine > (2026x) Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tool supports multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Supported scripting languages' space=''] you can use for expression specification. Additionally, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Supported scripting languages' space=''] you may need. However, if your project contains expressions that use a scripting language that is neither built-in nor manually added to the tool, it is considered missing in the tool; thus, the specified expressions cannot be executed, causing errors in the project.

##### Expression evaluation failure

For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed. Since, in this case, JavaScript Nashorn is neither built-in nor added manually to the modeling tool, the script cannot be executed, causing an evaluation error, as shown below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The error notification indicates the element containing the failed expression. Clicking the icon on the bottom right opens a Notification Window that displays the element name with the script that cannot be executed.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Expression evaluation failure notification in the Notification Window

The missing language is also indicated in the Expression dialog's Language field for scripts.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Missing language indication in the Expression dialog

##### Resolving expression evaluation failure

To resolve an expression evaluation failure, do one of the following

- Rewrite the script in a scripting language that is already supported by the modeling tool. To learn more about the built-in languages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Supported scripting languages' space=''] page.
- Add the missing language to the modeling tool. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The modeling tool supports multiple <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported scripting languages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[built-in scripting languages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you can use for expression specification. Additionally, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported scripting languages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[add other scripting languages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you may need. However, if your project contains expressions that use a scripting language that is neither built-in nor manually added to the tool, it is considered missing in the tool; thus, the specified expressions cannot be executed, causing errors in the project.</p><h3>Expression evaluation failure</h3><p>For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed. Since, in this case, JavaScript Nashorn is neither built-in nor added manually to the modeling tool, the script cannot be executed, causing an evaluation error, as shown below. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expression_evaluation_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Missing expression language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </h6><p>The error notification indicates the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">element containing the failed expression</span>. Clicking the icon on the bottom right opens a Notification Window that displays the element name with the script that cannot be executed. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="notification_window_expression_evaluation_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Missing expression language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Expression evaluation failure notification in the Notification Window</h6><p>The missing language is also indicated in the Expression dialog's Language field for scripts.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="missing_language_expression_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Missing expression language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Missing language indication in the Expression dialog</h6><h3>Resolving expression evaluation failure</h3><p>To resolve an expression evaluation failure, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Rewrite the script in a scripting language that is already supported by the modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ea17ebc-15ef-460f-b0f5-cbbe38882859"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about the built-in languages, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported scripting languages" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Add the missing language to the modeling tool. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de6f0ee7-9c62-4a58-b0b8-c4c1f29387c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool" /></ac:link> page. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul>
````

1487246060

1487246062

1487246061

**Description**

A Mission indicates the ongoing operational Activity of the enterprise. The Mission describes what the business is or will be doing on a day-to-day basis.

A Mission makes a Vision operative. It indicates the ongoing Activity that makes the Vision a reality. A Mission is planned by means of Strategies.

**Notation**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272728856 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03412: (2026x) MODAF

- page_id: `272728856`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728856/2026x+MODAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:32:47.038+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

According to modeling needs, there are the following MODAF templates for different purposes:

- **MODAF Project**- creating a MODAF project will switch the application to the MODAF graphical user interface and use the MODAF model templates.
- **MODAF Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new MODAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build the architecture by providing summary information about the MODAF structure and the architecture creation process.

All MODAF views are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 03414: (2026x) MODAF All Views Viewpoint

- page_id: `272729019`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729019/2026x+MODAF+All+Views+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:22.168+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411478012

411478014

411478013

Elements that are part of the All View. The All-Views (AVs) provide an overarching description of the architecture, its scope, ownership, timeframe and all of the other meta data that is required in order to effectively search and query architectural models. They also provide a place to record any findings arising from the architecting process. The AVs include a dictionary of the terms used in the construction of the architecture – which helps others fully understand its meaning at a later date. Since the AVs provide critical information for the future access and exploitation of an architectural model their population is essential whenever an architecture is created or modified. The AVs provide a critical input into the processes that provide architectural governance.

411478011

**Views**

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731927 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03415: (2026x) MODAF Operational Viewpoint

- page_id: `272731927`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731927/2026x+MODAF+Operational+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:50.940+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413846051

413846053

413846052

The Operational Viewpoint is about real-world activities, the people and machinery that perform them, and the means by which they are performed. The Operational Viewpoint is divided into nine views intended to answer the “who”, “what”, “when”, “where”, “why”, and “how” of a mission.

The Operational Views are common to MODAF and DoDAF; OV-1b, OV-1c, and OV-2 however, have been customized to provide for MOD requirements. The Operational Views describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to conduct operations. In MODAF thinking, the OV Views are considered to illustrate the Logical Architecture of the enterprise.

413846050

**View**

****

In MODAF thinking, the OV Views are considered to illustrate the Logical Architecture of the enterprise.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51021151-7b24-4f28-ab31-f3d730d0b90f"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8cbd928-d9b8-44d5-a428-f1c17ff35954"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413846050</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>View</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7c989114-0d1c-4195-a04c-eabf736fc9cc" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

417346698

417346697

The Services View (SOV) shows the elements that are part of SOV-1 through SOV-5.

The Service-Orientated View is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service is described as a unit of work through which a particular Resource provides a useful result to a consuming Resource.

417346695

**Views**

411757999

411758001

411758000

The Strategic View (StV) shows the elements that are part of StV-1 through StV-6. The Strategic Elements are used in the Strategic View that provides an overall Enterprise Architecture assessment of the Capabilities and their relationships facilitating Capability Management (e.g. capability introduction, integration, re-alignment and removal). While an Enterprise will have a number of UAF Architecture Descriptions that have the Operational, System, Technical Standards, and All Views, only one Strategic View will exist across a number of Architecture Descriptions.

411757997

**Views**

## PAGE 03418: (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint

419208934

419208936

419208935

The System View (SV) shows the elements that are part of SV-1 through SV-12. Models in the System Viewpoint represent alternate realizations in terms of equipment capability of the operational capabilities expressed through models in the Operational Viewpoint and in the User Requirements. The System Viewpoint primarily addresses the specification of the system capability needed (rather than implementation details). Significant changes originally made in MODAF improved the ability for modelers to represent configuration of capability that include people as well as systems and platforms.

419208933

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729057 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03419: (2026x) MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint

- page_id: `272729057`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729057/2026x+MODAF+Technical+Standards+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:30.611+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418729522

418729524

418729523

The Technical View (TV) shows the elements that are part of TV-1 through TV-2.

The Technical View is a set of views delineating standards, rules, notations, and conventions that apply to the implementation of the system architecture. When the standards profile is tied to the system elements to which they apply, TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to TV-2 standards forecasts in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).

MODAF extends the core DoDAF Technical Standards Views to include non-technical standards and policies applicable to the architecture such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc. Additionally, the TV-1 may also document policies and standards applicable to the operational or business context. MODAF also distinguishes between ‘applicability’ and ‘conformance’ with regard to architectural elements. If a standard is applicable to a given architecture, that architecture need not be fully conformant with the standard. The degree of conformance to a given standard may be judged on a risk basis at an approval point. An association between a Standard and an architectural element is not to be interpreted as stating the level of compliance of the element is fully compliant with that Standard. Additional evidences would need to be given (outside MODAF) to confirm the level of compliance. Finally, MODAF adds the explicit requirement that any Standards cited in TV-1 View must, where appropriate, be in accordance with the trend towards open architectures – i.e. standards which encourage stove-piped systems are expressly prohibited.

418729521

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

When the standards profile is tied to the system elements to which they apply, TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to TV-2 standards forecasts in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).</p><p>MODAF extends the core DoDAF Technical Standards Views to include non-technical standards and policies applicable to the architecture such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc. Additionally, the TV-1 may also document policies and standards applicable to the operational or business context. MODAF also distinguishes between ‘applicability’ and ‘conformance’ with regard to architectural elements. If a standard is applicable to a given architecture, that architecture need not be fully conformant with the standard. The degree of conformance to a given standard may be judged on a risk basis at an approval point. An association between a Standard and an architectural element is not to be interpreted as stating the level of compliance of the element is fully compliant with that Standard. Additional evidences would need to be given (outside MODAF) to confirm the level of compliance. Finally, MODAF adds the explicit requirement that any Standards cited in TV-1 View must, where appropriate, be in accordance with the trend towards open architectures – i.e. standards which encourage stove-piped systems are expressly prohibited.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eab04b60-7f6b-4780-b0ad-0b6684437e20"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418729521</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="256e754f-3204-47a1-bb84-748b710aaf8c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731920 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03420: (2026x) MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters

- page_id: `272731920`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731920/2026x+MODAF.+AcV-1+Acquisition+Clusters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:50.188+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413387346

413387349

413387348

**Description**

The Acquisition Clusters (AcV-1) View describes how acquisition projects are grouped in organisational terms as a coherent portfolio of acquisition programmes.

The AcV-1 View provides a way of describing the organizational relationships between multiple acquisition projects, each of which is responsible for delivering individual systems or capabilities. By definition, this View covers acquisition programmes consisting of multiple projects and will generally not be developed by those building Architectures for an individual project. In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.

The AcV-1 View is hierarchical in nature. Higher level groupings of projects (or, rather the organisations that own these projects) form acquisition clusters.

The intent of an AcV-1 View Product is to show:

- All of the acquisition projects delivering systems or system of systems (SoS) within the acquisition programmes under consideration.
- Other systems and SoS which may have a bearing on the Architecture.
- How the systems will be best integrated into acquisition clusters.
- The nesting of acquisition clusters to form a hierarchy.

**Implementation**

AcV-1 can be represented using:

- AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix

**Related views**

In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.

413387345

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afd1d3af-f4dc-467b-8fb2-9e2ca67cd031"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413387346</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c54ed0d1-ca28-4a36-91a5-eb7ab785a1b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413387349</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d3f4a99-74ed-4aa1-930f-994dcdf73953"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413387348</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Acquisition Clusters (AcV-1) View describes how acquisition projects are grouped in organisational terms as a coherent portfolio of acquisition programmes.</p><p>The AcV-1 View provides a way of describing the organizational relationships between multiple acquisition projects, each of which is responsible for delivering individual systems or capabilities. By definition, this View covers acquisition programmes consisting of multiple projects and will generally not be developed by those building Architectures for an individual project. In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.</p><p>The AcV-1 View is hierarchical in nature. Higher level groupings of projects (or, rather the organisations that own these projects) form acquisition clusters.</p><p>The intent of an AcV-1 View Product is to show:</p><ul><li>All of the acquisition projects delivering systems or system of systems (SoS) within the acquisition programmes under consideration.</li><li>Other systems and SoS which may have a bearing on the Architecture.</li><li>How the systems will be best integrated into acquisition clusters.</li><li>The nesting of acquisition clusters to form a hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AcV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Acv_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44dedf88-c1f1-4a3b-8275-806a92a0fde4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413387345</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Post" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Responsible For" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project Sequence" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project Milestone Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Status Indicators" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating AcV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building AcV-1 matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413597688

413597690

413597689

**Description**

The AcV-2 view provides a timeline perspective on programmes.

The AcV-2 View is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

For capability-based procurement, these work streams might conveniently be equated with Defence Lines of Development (DLODs).

**Implementation**

AcV-2 can be represented using an AcV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Acv-2 Programme Timelines

**Related views**

The use of AcV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the capability phasing view (StV-3), if one exists.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729021 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03422: (2026x) MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `272729021`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729021/2026x+MODAF.+AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:22.818+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF All Views Viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411544091

411544094

411544092

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. AV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the AV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.

**Implementation**

AV-1 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- Using a AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of AV-1 Overview and Summary Information report

411544089

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Description' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729031 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03423: (2026x) MODAF. AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `272729031`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729031/2026x+MODAF.+AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:24.957+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF All Views Viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411641246

411641249

411641248

**Description**

An AV-2 presents all the Elements used in an architecture as a standalone structure. An AV-2 presents all the Elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. An AV-2 shows elements from the Ontology that have been used in the architecture and new elements that have been introduced by the architecture. Architectures often introduce new terms – usually because the architecture is covering new technology or business processes. The purpose of the AV-2 is to provide a local extension of the Ontology to explain the terms and abbreviations used in building the architecture.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data.
- An AV-2 table. If you have AV-2 diagrams from previous versions, they will be migrated into the AV-1 diagram when loading the project with UPDM 17.0.2.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of AV-2 Integrated Dictionary report

411641245

## PAGE 03424: (2026x) MODAF. OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

- page_id: `272731929`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731929/2026x+MODAF.+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:51.170+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413963572

413963574

413963573

**Description**

The purpose of High-level Operational Concept Graphic is to provide a high-level graphical and textual description of operational concept (high level organizations, missions, geographic configuration, connectivity, etc) of what the architecture is supposed to do, and how it is supposed to do it. The OV-1, along with the corresponding AV-1 product is intended to serve as an executive summary of the architecture.

**Implementation**

OV-1 can be represented using:

- Link to an external document.
- OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure Diagram.

**Sample** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

413963571
